# GEVIBench — Full Deep-Audit Sweep Report

**Workflow:** `gevi-fix-sweep` (deep paper-reading audit → auto-fix → digitize → Sonnet QA)  
**Date:** 2026-07-03  
**Run ID:** `wf_133b3200-94e` (initial task `w66prpcq0` + resume `wsh8ol06i`)  
**Scope:** all 73 GEVI pages — **complete**  
**Effort:** ~240 agent invocations · ~14.2M subagent tokens · ~5,700 tool calls · ~130 min wall time (across 2 sessions)  
**Backup (rollback point):** `.gevi-backups/pre-sweep-2026-07-03/` (all 73 JSONs snapshotted before the run)  
**Git baseline:** `468aecd` on `master`

> ✅ **All 73 GEVIs audited and QA-checked.** The initial run hit the account session limit at 54/73; it was resumed after the reset (cached agents returned instantly) to finish the remaining 19 fix agents and back-fill every skipped QA pass. All 73 JSON files re-validated — **all parse correctly** (no corruption). The Sonnet QA phase independently re-checked every applied edit against the primary source; **19 pages carry at least one QA-flagged item to review** (see §QA Findings).

> **Two-run provenance note:** 3 pages (`quasar2`, `quasar3`, `restus2s`) were killed mid-edit in the first run (edits landed on disk but no report). The resume re-audited all three against the papers: `quasar3` (F-V curve re-digitized to the correct paQuasAr3 trace) and `restus2s` (2 spurious spectrum values removed) verified **clean**; `quasar2` got one additional fix. Their run-1 edits are therefore verified-correct but itemized only via the git/backup diff, not always in a `changesApplied` row.

## Executive Summary

| Outcome | Count |
|---|---|
| ✔ Audited — clean, no changes needed | 20 |
| 🟢 Audited — fixed | 39 |
| 🟢 Audited — fixed, with open items | 14 |
| **Total audited** | **73** |
| ⏸ Never ran (session limit), file untouched | 0 |
| ⚠️ Never ran but **partial edits landed** (unreported) | 0 |
| **Total not completed** | **0** |

**Aggregate edits actually written to disk (across all 73 audited pages):**

- **135** value/structural changes applied
- **34** curves digitized (photobleach / F-V / spectrum / apWidth)
- **81** cross-GEVI TODOs deferred (need serial application — see §Cross-GEVI)
- **123** unresolved / judgment-call items flagged (see §Your Call)

**QA status:** every edited page was independently QA-checked against its primary source. 19 pages have **flagged issues** to review (`archer2`, `archon2`, `asap3`, `cepheid1b`, `electraoff`, `electraon`, `electricpk`, `flicr1`, `flicr2`, `hvi`, `macq-morange2`, `nirbutterfly`, `pacer`, `positron2`, `props`, `quasar2`, `quasar6`, `solaris`, `vsfpbutterfly`); the rest passed all checks.

## Remaining Follow-Ups (not blockers — the audit itself is complete)

The sweep finished all 73 pages; these are the *deferred/optional* items it surfaced for you to decide on:

1. **Review the QA-flagged pages** (see §QA Findings) — a handful of applied edits the QA agent judged wrong, unsupported, or a mis-read. These are the highest-value things to look at.
2. **Apply the Cross-GEVI TODOs** (see §Cross-GEVI) — data one paper reports about *other* sensors; agents may only edit their own file, so none of these are applied yet.
3. **Resolve the Your-Call items** (see §Your Call) — judgment calls the agents deliberately did not guess on.
4. **Digitizations landed unreviewed** — per the workflow's design, QA flags bad curves but does not undo them; spot-check any t75/FWHM/F-V you rely on against its overlay image (digitization scatter ~±20–25%).

## Cross-GEVI TODOs (deferred — apply serially)

Agents may only edit their own JSON, so data a paper reports about *other* sensors is deferred here. **None of these have been applied yet.**

**from `2photron`:**
- → `jedi2p` · `apWidthData`: Add optical AP FWHM 1.84 ± 0.46 ms [1.25-3.55], modality 2P, sample 'Mouse, V1 cortex layer II/III, awake behaving, in vivo (JEDI-2P-Kv)', samplingRate ~3500 (ULoVE >3.5 kHz; note the value is from Liu et al. 2022 cross-plotted in this paper), source doi:10.1101/2024.11.15.623698, sourceFigure 'Fig. 3f', proofread false. Numeric value stated in main text.
- → `asap3` · `apWidthData`: Add optical AP FWHM ~3.4 ms (read from Fig. 3f grey bar; no numeric value in text), modality 2P, sample 'Mouse, cortex, in vivo (ASAP3)', samplingRate ~3500 (ULoVE >3.5 kHz; data from Villette et al. 2019 cross-plotted here), source doi:10.1101/2024.11.15.623698, sourceFigure 'Fig. 3f', proofread false. NOTE: bar-chart read, confirm before storing.

**from `ace2n-mneon2`:**
- → `ace2n-mneon` · `photobleach`: Fig. S11B (Science 2022, doi:10.1126/science.abm8797) plots Ace-mNeon (dark-green curve) alongside Ace-mNeon2 & pAce, HEK, continuous 505 nm 25 mW/mm², n=4 wells — add a matching photobleach[] entry (same source + sourceFigure 'Fig. S11B', own sourceImage) so all three cross-overlay. (Parent may already have a figs11b entry — verify before adding.)
- → `pace` · `photobleach`: Same Fig. S11B (Science 2022): pAce is the light-blue/cyan curve — add matching photobleach[] entry (same source + 'Fig. S11B', own sourceImage) for cross-overlay, if a pace page exists.
- → `ace2n-mneon` · `dynamicRangeData / kinetics`: Table S1 (Science 2022) gives Ace-mNeon (Ace1a, from Kannan 2018): ΔF/F per 100-mV −11.6%, activation fast amp 60%/τf 0.81/τs 4.6, deactivation 60%/τf 0.77/τs 5.2 (amplitudes approximate, noted with asterisk). Verify parent's stored values against this table row if not already sourced from it.

**from `archer1`:**
- → `quasar2` · `photobleach`: Add photobleach[] entry from Piatkevich 2018 Fig 2i (QuasAr2 = black SOLID curve), source doi:10.1038/s41589-018-0004-9, sourceFigure 'Fig. 2i', own sourceImage public/photobleach-sources/quasar2-fig2i.jpg, modality 1P, illumination '2200 mW/mm²' (637nm 2.2 W/mm², 333 Hz), intensityMWmm2 OMITTED (panel not iso-condition). Curve ~100→~93@100s→~72@480s→~49@940s; will cross-overlay Archer1 via matching source+sourceFigure.
- → `archon1` · `photobleach`: Add photobleach[] entry from Piatkevich Fig 2i (Archon1 = magenta, most photostable, ~100→~93@940s), source doi:10.1038/s41589-018-0004-9, sourceFigure 'Fig. 2i', own sourceImage, modality 1P, illumination '800 mW/mm²' (637nm), intensityMWmm2 OMITTED. Likely never reaches 75% in-window → omit t75, note honest lower bound.
- → `archon2` · `photobleach`: Add photobleach[] entry from Piatkevich Fig 2i (Archon2 = blue, ~100→~55@940s), source doi:10.1038/s41589-018-0004-9, sourceFigure 'Fig. 2i', own sourceImage, modality 1P, illumination '800 mW/mm²' (637nm), intensityMWmm2 OMITTED.
- → `ace2n-mneon` · `photobleach`: Add photobleach[] entry from Piatkevich Fig 2i (Ace = green curve = Ace2N-4aa-mNeon, fastest bleach ~100→~19@640s), source doi:10.1038/s41589-018-0004-9, sourceFigure 'Fig. 2i', own sourceImage, modality 1P, illumination '13 mW/mm²' (475/34BP LED — DIFFERENT wavelength than the red opsins), intensityMWmm2 OMITTED. Verify which ace2n-mneon page id corresponds (this is the mNeonGreen FP channel, 1P widefield).

**from `archon1`:**
- → `asap1` · `apWidthData`: Add optical AP FWHM 8.5±0.2 ms (n=3) from Milosevic 2020 eNeuro Fig. 1D, samplingRate 500 Hz, sample 'Mouse, cultured hippocampal neurons, in vitro', modality 1P, source doi:10.1523/ENEURO.0060-20.2020, note it was cross-measured in a study whose primary subject is a comparison of several GEVIs, proofread:false
- → `asap2s` · `apWidthData`: Add optical AP FWHM 12.3±0.7 ms (n=6) from Milosevic 2020 eNeuro Fig. 1B/D (paper labels it 'ASAP2'), samplingRate 500 Hz, sample 'Mouse, cultured hippocampal neurons, in vitro', modality 1P, source doi:10.1523/ENEURO.0060-20.2020, proofread:false. NOTE: verify ASAP2 vs ASAP2s attribution — paper uses AAV CamK2-ASAP2s / hSyn-ASAP2s (Fig 1B legend) so it is ASAP2s.
- → `bongwoori` · `apWidthData`: Add optical AP FWHM 33.1±2.6 ms (n=6) from Milosevic 2020 eNeuro Fig. 1C/D. NOTE: the paper used Bongwoori-Pos6 (hSyn-Bongwoori-Pos6) — attribute to the Bongwoori-Pos6/relevant variant page, not plain Bongwoori, if a separate page exists. samplingRate 500 Hz, sample 'Mouse, cultured hippocampal neurons, in vitro', modality 1P, source doi:10.1523/ENEURO.0060-20.2020, proofread:false

**from `archon2`:**
- → `archon1` · `spectrum (peakEm + custom.emission + custom.minEm + note)`: archon1 shares the IDENTICAL mis-calibrated Song 2024 Fig 1b emission: peakEm stored 693 (should be 670), minEm 650 (should be 651), 121-pt curve peaking at 693 with the same artifactual noisy far-red tail crashing to ~0.0025. Replace with the corrected 120-pt curve (minEm 651, peak 670, smooth decline to 0.14) available at scratchpad/archon2_emission.json, set peakEm=670, and update the note to match archon2's corrected note (note the Song lambda-stack ~670 nm peak vs ~710 nm elsewhere). Set spectrum.proofread=false.

**from `asap2s`:**
- → `asap3` · `apWidthData (Fig 2f entry, fwhm 5.8 ms)`: Review/remove the Fig 2f-derived apWidth entry (source doi:10.1038/s41467-023-41975-3, sourceFigure 'Fig. 2f'). Fig 2f plots 'Width at 30% maximum response', NOT FWHM — same defect removed from asap2s. ASAP3's actual Fig 2f x-value is ~8 ms anyway, not 5.8. The JEDI-1P paper provides no true optical single-AP FWHM for ASAP3.
- → `jedi1p` · `apWidthData (Fig 2f entry, fwhm 4.9 ms)`: Review/remove the Fig 2f-derived apWidth entry (sourceFigure 'Fig. 2f'). Fig 2f is 'Width at 30% maximum response', not FWHM. JEDI-1P's true optical-AP FWHM should come from a real half-max waveform panel if one exists, not this 30%-max scatter plot.
- → `asap2s (brightnessData — flag only, do NOT auto-edit)` · `brightnessData asap3 ratio 1.11`: Verify the direction of {ratio:1.11, reference:'asap3'}. JEDI-2P Fig 2I shows ASAP2s dimmer than ASAP3 under 2P (920 nm), so ASAP2s/ASAP3 should be <1; the stored 1.11 may be inverted. Needs a careful Fig 2I bar-height read before changing.

**from `asap3`:**
- → `asap2s` · `apWidthData`: Add optical spike-width entry: fwhm≈17.3 ms, modality 1P, sample 'Cultured rat hippocampal neurons, optical single-AP response, current clamp, room temperature', temperature 25°C, source doi:10.1016/j.cell.2019.11.004, sourceFigure 'Fig. 2F', samplingRate omitted (paper reports only 1–2 kHz range; kinetics-dominated), note that it was co-plotted with ASAP3 (12.7 ms) in the ASAP3 origin paper (Villette 2019, primary subject ASAP3), proofread:false.

**from `asap5`:**
- → `voltron2` · `photobleach`: Fig S5E of the ASAP5 Neuron 2024 paper (doi:10.1016/j.neuron.2024.08.019) plots Voltron2525-Kv (JF525 dye) alongside ASAP5-Kv (F/F0 vs Time, 0-60s). Rulebook 1i would add a matching photobleach entry to voltron2 for cross-overlay. CAVEAT/JUDGMENT CALL: ASAP5-Kv's time-axis in this panel is rescaled ×2.3 (SNR-matched to effective ~20 mW/mm²) while Voltron2525-Kv's is NOT (plotted at 46 mW/mm²), so the two curves are on different effective time bases — a naive same-source+sourceFigure cross-overlay would misrepresent Voltron2's bleaching. Recommend either (a) skip, or (b) add Voltron2525-Kv digitized at its true 46 mW/mm² with a note that the two curves are NOT on a common time base. Do NOT set proofread:true.

**from `asap7y`:**
- → `asap3` · `photobleach`: Add a photobleach[] entry from the ASAP7y paper's Ext. Data Fig. 3a (source doi:10.64898/2026.05.27.728040, sourceFigure 'Ext. Fig. 3a', modality 1P, ~10 µW/mm² 445 nm CW, in vivo PV interneurons). ASAP3 (green) is co-plotted with ASAP7y and drops ~40% over 1 h (vs ~10% for ASAP7y) with a sawtooth from periodic 445-nm recovery bouts (reversible photobleaching). Digitizing needs continuity-tracking to skip the recovery jumps; the trace reaches ~65% at ~55 min. Own sourceImage under public/photobleach-sources/asap3-ext-fig3a.jpg. This makes ASAP3 and ASAP7y cross-overlay on the Ext. Fig. 3a photobleach panel.
- → `asap5` · `photobleach (optional)`: Optional: Fig. 2g plots normalized fluorescence of ASAP5 vs ASAP7y over 1 min of continuous 2P imaging (Mi1, 1030 nm). Could add stand-alone 2P (power-only, no intensityMWmm2) photobleach entries for both ASAP5 and ASAP7y from Fig. 2g so they cross-overlay. Lower priority than the ASAP3 1P entry.

**from `bongwoori`:**
- → `bongwoori-r3` · `spectrum.peakEx`: Set peakEx 490→488 for consistency with the stored FPbase EGFP curve (peaks at 489) and the sibling convention (arclight/marina/pado = 488). Same cosmetic fix applied to bongwoori this pass; not applied to bongwoori-r3 here to avoid parallel-write clobber.

**from `cepheid1b`:**
- → `cepheid1s` · `apWidthData`: Add apWidthData entry: {fwhm: 7.9, samplingRate: 484, sample: "cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro", modality: "1P", source: "doi:10.1126/sciadv.adi4208", sourceFigure: "Fig. 1D", proofread: false, note: 'Optical single-AP FWHM derived by digitizing the averaged Cepheid1s optical waveform in Fig. 1D (20-ms scale bar; half baseline-to-peak span ≈7.5 px → ~7.9 ms). 484 Hz widefield 1P current-clamp in cultured hippocampal neurons; kinetics-dominated. No numeric FWHM stated; plot-derived.'}
- → `varnam2` · `apWidthData`: Add apWidthData entry: {fwhm: 8.8, samplingRate: 484, sample: "cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro", modality: "1P", source: "doi:10.1126/sciadv.adi4208", sourceFigure: "Fig. 1D", proofread: false, note: 'Optical single-AP FWHM derived by digitizing the VARNAM2 optical waveform in Fig. 1D of the Cepheid paper (Han 2023; 20-ms scale bar; half-max span ≈8.4 px → ~8.8 ms). 484 Hz widefield 1P current-clamp, cultured hippocampal neurons; kinetics-dominated. No numeric FWHM stated; plot-derived. Measured in a paper whose primary subjects are Cepheid1b/1s.'}

**from `cepheid1s`:**
- → `cepheid1b` · `photobleach[0]`: Consistency check (do not necessarily change): cepheid1b/varnam/varnam2 share Fig. S6A. Their photobleach t75 handling may warrant the same no-unanchored-extrapolation review, but cepheid1b/varnam curves DO reach 75% in-window so their t75 are likely fine. Verify none carries a spurious extrapolated t75.

**from `electraoff`:**
- → `pace` · `apWidthData`: Add apWidthData entry from Fig. 1j (averaged single-AP optical waveform, cyan pAce trace) — source doi:10.1101/2025.05.30.657123, sourceFigure 'Fig. 1j', samplingRate 1000, sample 'Mouse, primary hippocampal neurons, in vitro', modality '1P', temperature '22°C'. FWHM needs careful isolated digitization (sibling traces overlap gray single-sweeps in the small panel; my noisy estimate ~5.5 ms is NOT reliable — re-digitize per Step 1j).
- → `electraon` · `apWidthData`: Add apWidthData entry from Fig. 1j (orange ElectraON averaged spike waveform), same source/rate/sample/modality/temp as above. FWHM re-digitize (my noisy estimate ~4.8 ms, unreliable due to overlap).

**`2photron`:**
- **apWidthData (Golgi-cell prep)** — Fig. S9d reports a separate cerebellar-Golgi-cell FWHM for 2Photron (~0.64 ms), plus JEDI-2P (~0.72 ms) and Voltron2 (~0.87 ms)  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Sub-millisecond value read from a small bar chart, below the rulebook 1-20 ms sanity floor; imprecise read risks a low-quality entry. The explicit-in-text cortical 1.42 ms is the solid apWidth. Left for a human decision on whether to digitize Fig. S9d bars precisely.
- **kinetics[0].temperature** — Stored '25°C' but the paper never states the in-vitro voltage-clamp recording temperature  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Room-temperature-→25°C is the rulebook convention and a reasonable default for cultured-neuron voltage clamp; changing/removing it would be a guess. Value kept; flagged for awareness.
- **addgene** — No addgene entry  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Optional field; no web access this run to confirm a plasmid deposit for a 2024 preprint.
- **spectrum (FPbase re-verification)** — Could not re-query FPbase this run (no WebFetch; curl blocked) to byte-verify the JF552-HaloTag conjugate curve  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Curve is real (asymmetric, vibronic), already sourced+sourceFigure'd; accepted on prior provenance and structural checks rather than fabricate a verification.

**`ace2n-mneon`:**
- **dynamicRangeData[0]** — The list column labelled 'ΔF/F per 100 mV (steady-state)' displays entry[0] = the −17.9% PEAK value verbatim (GEVIList shows entry[0], not the median), so a peak number appears under a steady-state header.

Reordering/removing entry[0] would change the headline number — a judgment call the prior curation deliberately left as-is. Fixing belongs in GEVIList.tsx (out of scope for this own-file-only audit).
- **researchPapers** — No exhaustive citation crawl performed to confirm the 6-paper list is complete.

**`ace2n-mneon2`:**
- **sensitivityData[0].note** — Note attributes the −16.5±1.9%/spike value to Drosophila PPL1-γ2α'1 dopaminergic recordings and explicitly says 'not the V1 VIP+ mouse traces shown in Fig. 1I' — but the Science 2022 main text (line 419) places −16.5% (n=127 spikes) with the mouse V1 VIP+ interneuron single-AP measurement (Fig. 1, H and I). The entry's own sourceFigure 'Fig. 1I' is the mouse panel, so the note contradicts the sourceFigure and the paper.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Entry is proofread:true; Step 0 forbids modifying human-locked entries. Value (−16.5%) and sourceFigure ('Fig. 1I') are both correct — only the prose sample attribution is wrong. Left for the human to correct the note.
- **dynamicRangeData[1]** — sourceFigure is 'Table S2' but the note says the value (−26.7±3.2%/100mV) is the 'main text Fig. 1C value' from the Vega paper (doi:10.1101/2025.05.29.656768). Internal figure/table label inconsistency.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Vega paper is not available locally; cannot verify which label is authoritative. Changing sourceFigure without the source would be a guess.
- **photobleach[0] (Fig. S4h, mBaoJin/Electra)** — Color-to-sensor assignment (Ace-mNeon2 = green curve) rests on cross-referencing labeled bars in Fig. S4f/g per the note; the mBaoJin paper is not available locally to independently confirm the green trace is Ace-mNeon2.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Cannot open the source paper to re-verify the color legend; entry is internally documented and proofread:false. Flagging rather than assuming the assignment is wrong.

**`arch`:**
- **brightnessData** — Kralj 2012 reports WT Arch fluorescence QY ≈ 9×10⁻⁴ (main text); stored value uses Herasymenko 2025 QY 1.1×10⁻⁴ (~8× difference), giving ratio 0.00016 vs ~0.0013 with the Kralj QY.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Both are legitimate primary measurements from different methods; not a fabrication. Stored value is internally consistent and explicitly cited to Herasymenko. Choosing between them is a judgment call for the human.
- **apWidthData** — FWHM 4.3 ms is plot-derived from a small figure inset, not a paper-stated number.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Not an error — this is the sanctioned derive-from-plot route (rulebook 1j) and was overlay-verified. Noted so the human knows it is a digitized value with ~±0.3 ms reading uncertainty.

**`archer1`:**
- **kinetics[0].temperature** — Recording temperature missing (trips checker WARNING)

- **kinetics[0].on/off** — 2.5/5.5 ms is LOW-confidence bar-chart digitization (±~1 ms) and is the ONLY numeric kinetics source  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Bando 2019 PDF not local to re-verify the bars; origin Fig 1e gives only normalized waveforms (no numeric τ) and Piatkevich lists 'No data available'. The documented derivation is self-consistent; altering without the source would reduce reliability.
- **apWidthData** — No optical single-AP FWHM for Archer1 in any available paper  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Origin paper states none and Fig 3e is a 40 Hz AP train (not a clean averaged single-AP waveform); Piatkevich Fig 2h FWHMAP is Archon1-only with no cross-sensor Archer1 value. Genuinely absent — nothing to extract or derive.

**`archer2`:**
- **addgene** — No Addgene ID stored; Archer1 sibling uses 60423 and an adjacent Gradinaru-lab deposit for Archer2 likely exists.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Could not confirm the exact Archer2 plasmid ID — curl to addgene.org was permission-blocked and no WebFetch tool was available. Guessing an ID would be fabrication; the original paper does not print an Addgene number.
- **kinetics** — Archer2 τon/τoff are absent (paper only says Archer2 has a 'slower rise to steady-state', Fig. 1c, qualitative).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ No numeric Archer2 kinetics are published in any available paper. Flytzanis 2014 gives no τ table; Bando 2019 (source of Archer1's kinetics) characterized Archer1, not Archer2. Fabricating from the Archer1 bars would be wrong.
- **sensitivityData** — No Archer2 per-AP ΔF/F stored.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The 25-40% per-AP figure in the paper is explicitly for Archer1; no numeric Archer2 per-AP value is published. Not fabricated.

**`archon1`:**
- **voltage.sourceFigure** — Labeled 'Fig. 2d' but the ΔF/F-vs-membrane-potential curve (the data actually stored in voltage.custom) is panel 2e; 2d is the raw voltage-step fluorescence traces.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The voltage object is proofread:true (deep verification/re-digitization skipped per rulebook). The stored sourceImage shows BOTH panels d and e, so the label is not misleading a user. Recommend a human relabel to 'Fig. 2e' but I did not modify a proofread-locked entry.
- **kinetics** — eNeuro Fig. 2E reports Archon1 ON 'Fast TAU' = 8.1±0.9 ms (n=4, HEK293, double-exp fit).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ It is the FAST component only; the paper's extracted text gives no slow-component τ or component amplitudes, so an amplitude-weighted mean cannot be computed. Storing the fast component alone violates the kinetics rule, and the metric is already covered by two properly-weighted entries. Deliberately omitted; flagged for awareness.
- **dynamicRangeData / sensitivityData / twoPhoton (Villette 2019 entries)** — deltaF 81% (DR), 30% (sens) from Villette 2019 Cell 'Table 1', and twoPhoton compatible:false, could not be opened directly (paper not local; PMC table not fetched).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Confirmed via web that the correct source paper is the ASAP3-Kv 2P study (PMC6941988) whose comparison table lists Archon/SomArchon as poor 2P performers, and the ΔF/F magnitudes match the literature and this page's other entries — so the values are plausible and correctly attributed, but the exact Table 1 cell values were not eyeballed from the primary PDF. Left as proofread:false verified-plausible.

**`archon2`:**
- **voltage.proofread** — Set to true (by a prior human/audit), which the checker itself must never set. Verified correct against Fig S16c but left as-is.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ I did not modify the voltage entry, so per the Step-0 skip rule I do not flip proofread. Flipping a human-approved true→false is a human decision; flagged for review.
- **brightnessData[0].reference** — Stored reference 'arch' but Monakhov Table 1 footnote b defines the brightness anchor as Arch(D95N), a distinct construct. The 80× value is correct.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ No 'arch-d95n' page exists; 'arch' is the site's existing brightness-graph anchor and a pre-existing convention across pages. Changing the anchor id is a site-wide judgment call, not a per-page fix.

**`asap1`:**
- **kinetics[1] (eLife 44.0/66.9)** — Entry is proofread:true, so per the rulebook I skipped deep re-verification. The eLife paper is local and its Table 1 amplitudes could be re-checked in a forced pass.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ proofread:true is human-locked; the instructions forbid changing such entries or setting proofread. No error suspected — the value is internally consistent with St-Pierre (weighted) and Zou.
- **photobleach (St-Pierre Supp Fig 9)** — nn.3709 Supp Fig 9 is a photobleaching figure but is a per-neuron scatter (4 cells, colored markers, imaged every 10 s over varying 4.5-15 min durations), not a clean averaged F/F0 curve.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Not cleanly digitizable and already represented by photostabilityData[0] (74% @ 0.12 mW/mm², 15 min, Fig S9); adding a fabricated averaged curve would be low-value and risk misrepresentation. Left as an INFO gap.
- **spectrum.sourceFigure / photobleach[0].sourceFigure format** — eLife figure supplements are labeled 'Fig. 1—figure supplement 3A', which is not the rulebook's strict 'Fig. S3A' supplementary format.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The eLife-native label is unambiguous and is used identically across the 3 cross-overlaying GEVIs (asap1/asap2s/arclight); the photobleach cross-overlay matches on identical source+sourceFigure, so renaming would silently break the overlay. Judgment call left to human.

**`asap2s`:**
- **brightnessData[0] (asap3, ratio 1.11, Fig 2I)** — Direction likely inverted: Fig 2I (JEDI-2P paper) shows brightness ordering JEDI-2P > ASAP3 > ASAP2s under 2P at 920 nm, so ASAP2s/ASAP3 should be <1, but the stored ratio is 1.11 (ASAP2s = 1.11× ASAP3).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Correcting requires precise digitization of the Fig 2I bar heights (a violin/bar panel); I did not want to substitute a guessed value and risk introducing a new error. Flagged for a careful figure read.
- **photobleach[0] (JEDI-1P Fig S4, 46 mW/mm²)** — The stored ASAP2s custom[] plateaus perfectly flat at 0.7086 for the final 12 points, which is a digitization simplification of the real slowly-declining relative-brightness trace.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ No metric (t75/t50) is derived from this entry — it is retained only for cross-overlay — so the flat tail is cosmetically imperfect but harmless. Re-digitizing a tiny 5-panel relative-brightness inset is low-value; left as-is per the entry's stated purpose.

**`asap3`:**
- **parentId** — parentId is 'asap2s' but the origin paper states ASAP3 = ASAP2f + L146G/S147T/N149R/S150G/H151D/R414Q while ASAP2s = ASAP2f + R415Q — i.e. ASAP3 and ASAP2s are siblings both derived from ASAP2f, so asap2s is not ASAP3's molecular parent.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The true direct parent ASAP2f has no file in src/gevis/. Repointing parentId to a non-existent id would break the family-tree build; the alternative (creating an asap2f node and re-parenting the ASAP branch, or choosing a deliberate nearest-ancestor) is a structural change beyond this single-file audit and a human judgment call.
- **twoPhoton[0].sourceFigure** — Empty sourceFigure on the twoPhoton entry.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Field is optional and the entry is proofread:true (human-locked); 2P compatibility is established across the whole Cell 2019 paper (Fig. 3 / in-vivo 2P) rather than one panel, so no single figure label is uniquely correct.

**`asap4b`:**
- **photobleach (2P)** — Ext Fig 4b/c show 2P photobleaching for ASAP4b, but the paper states most 2P photobleaching is reversible photoactivation/recovery  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ 2P power-only, recovery-dominated curve is not a comparable/clean bleach; digitizing it would misrepresent photostability. Judgment call to leave for human decision.
- **photobleach (Fig 2b) t75=59s** — Very noisy in-vivo trace; the blue ASAP4b curve dips toward 0.6-0.8 across 20-60s so a strict first-crossing of 0.75 could be read earlier than 59s  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Stored smoothed digitization crosses 0.75 at ~59s consistent with its own data and fit; re-digitizing a noisy in-vivo trace by eye would not clearly improve accuracy. Left as-is (matches prior curated value).

**`asap5`:**
- **dynamicRangeData[1] (2P) / brightnessData[1]** — 2P dynamicRange −35.2% (Ext. Fig. 1b) and the asap4e brightness context are sourced from Nature Methods 2026 (doi:10.1038/s41592-026-03043-8, Land et al.) and the ASAP4 bioRxiv, neither of which is in the local Papers folder (Nature Methods is paywalled).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Could not open the primary source to independently re-verify; the value is internally consistent with the jedi3-audit's verified readings of the same Ext. Fig. 1b (JEDI-2P −41.78 ≈ the −41.8% reference in this entry's note), so it is most likely correct and was left unchanged rather than altered on a guess. Both entries are already proofread:false.
- **photobleach (Voltron2525-Kv cross-GEVI)** — Fig S5E co-plots Voltron2525-Kv, which rulebook 1i says should get a matching photobleach entry for cross-overlay.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Scope forbids editing other GEVI JSONs (recorded in crossGeviTODO); and it is a genuine judgment call because ASAP5-Kv's trace in this panel is ×2.3 time-rescaled while Voltron2's is not, so a same-panel cross-overlay would be misleading. Needs the human to decide (a) skip vs (b) add with a differing-time-base caveat.

**`asap6c`:**
- **parentId** — parentId=asap4b is a distant ancestor; true direct parent per the paper's engineering path is ASAP6.2/ASAP6.1 (cpGFP-S4 linker screen of the ASAP6.x leads).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The direct parent (ASAP6.2 = ASAP6b, and ASAP6.1) has no page in src/gevis/, so per rulebook 1h the grandparent/ancestor fallback (asap4b) is allowed and correct. Creating an asap6b page and re-parenting is a curator decision outside this single-file audit scope.

**`asap7y`:**
- **kinetics[0]/[1] on/off** — Stored τ are the dominant component (74-95% amplitude) of a biexponential fit; the slow-component τ is not reported anywhere in the paper, so a true amplitude-weighted mean τ̄ cannot be computed.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Rule 1b requires the amplitude-weighted mean for multi-exp fits, but computing it requires the slow τ, which is unpublished. Fabricating a slow τ or assuming 50:50 (the amplitudes ARE reported as 74-95% fast, contradicting 50:50) would be wrong. Kept the dominant τ (which the main text also cites) and documented the situation in notes.
- **photobleach[0].t75 / extrapolated** — t75=12570 s is extrapolated ~3.5× beyond the 60-min data with only a weak anchor ('~10% bleach at 1 h'); the 1P dose-scaling to 100 mW/mm² spans 4 orders of magnitude.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The entry is already flagged extrapolated:true with a thorough uncertainty note and treated as order-of-magnitude. Whether to keep the extrapolated t75 or switch to the rulebook's honest lower-bound (omit t75, note the bound) is a judgment call the human previously made in favor of keeping it; not overriding a documented, honestly-flagged choice.

**`bongwoori`:**
- **sensitivityData[1] (deltaF 2.5, Piao Fig 7A)** — 2.5% per-AP ΔF/F is an eyeballed estimate from a single-trial neuron trace whose only calibration is a -4% scale bar; Piao 2015 prints no numeric per-AP value  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ It represents a real (if imprecise) 1P per-AP measurement and I must not fabricate a replacement number; the tabulated Lee Table 3 3.7% coexists as the preferred value. Kept with a clarifying note — whether to drop the approximate 2.5% entry is a human judgment call.

**`cepheid1b`:**
- **sensitivityData** — Paper also reports Cepheid1b-ST -9.9±0.3% per AP @498 Hz in acute brain slice (Fig. 3B), a different prep from the stored -12.1% culture value (Fig. 1C).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Judgment call — the -12.1% culture value is the canonical characterization; adding the slice ST value is optional and would mix preps. Left to human to decide whether to add as a second entry.
- **spectrum.peakEm** — Declared peakEm 600 nm vs the custom curve's emission peak at 592 nm (the mScarlet-I FPbase profile used as the mScarlet-I1.4 stand-in).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Documented in the existing spectrum note; changing either would misrepresent the source (paper tabulates 600; the real mScarlet-I profile peaks ~592). Cosmetic, not a data error.

**`cepheid1s`:**
- **photobleach[0].t75** — Curve reaches only 77% at end of window; 75% crossing is ~24% beyond data (~948 s by biexp). Paper confirms high photostability (t50>13min) but gives no explicit t75 anchor.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Judgment call between the rulebook's honest-lower-bound default (chosen: omit t75) and a modest anchored extrapolation (t75≈948s, extrapolated:true). Left the conservative choice and flagged for human preference.
- **voltage.custom** — Cepheid1s (gray) and Cepheid1b (salmon) F-V curves in Fig 1B nearly coincide at the bottom; stored +40 point (−34) matches Cepheid1s −33.6% but could be within a pixel of the Cepheid1b −32.9% curve.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Difference is <1% and within digitization error; endpoints are anchored to the distinct main-text values (−33.6% for 1s). Not re-digitized — stored curve is correct within tolerance.

**`electraoff`:**
- **apWidthData (cross-GEVI pAce/ElectraON/Ace-mNeon2)** — Fig 1j plots all four sensors' averaged single-AP waveforms; rulebook 1j requires an apWidthData entry in every co-plotted GEVI  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Scope limits me to electraoff.json (parallel-clobber avoidance). Also, the sibling traces overlap gray individual sweeps in the small panel and my per-color masks were contaminated → I could not extract FWHM values I could stand behind. Deferred to crossGeviTODO for careful serial digitization rather than handing off unverified numbers.
- **spectrum** — ElectraOFF spectrum is the FPbase mBaoJin curve (paper publishes no ex/em); stated peaks 500/508 nm not independently re-verified against FPbase this session  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ curl/API access to fpbase.org was blocked in this environment; the stored curve is internally consistent (array peaks = stated peaks), asymmetric/real, correctly sourced, and shared verbatim with ElectraON/Vega per prior audits — no evidence of fabrication, so left as-is rather than guess.

**`electraon`:**
- **photostabilityData (Fig. 1h) brightnessRemaining** — Set to 100 because the digitized ElectraON Fig. 1h curve genuinely stays ~100-106% (no net bleaching). Rulebook 1e normally flags exactly-100 as suspicious.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The value is correct per the figure (the eFRET baseline shows a slight photoactivation rise then plateau, not decay), but it is unusual. Alternative is to drop this second photostabilityData entry entirely and rely on S4h (98%, 5 mW/mm², neurons). Left for human to decide keep-100-with-note vs drop-entry.
- **apWidthData[0].samplingRate** — Stored 1000 Hz is inferred (Fig. 1g labels the spike-imaging '1 kHz'; neuron imaging = same setup as HeLa) but is NOT explicitly stated for Fig. 1j, and ElectraON is fast (τon 1.11 / τoff 0.58 ms) so the measured FWHM (2.5 ms) is partly frame-rate-set.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Rulebook 1j says never invent a rate and only omit for slow kinetics-dominated widths (FWHM ≫ frame period); here 2.5 ms vs 1 ms is borderline. Chose to store 1000 Hz with an explicit caveat rather than omit (which would also be defensible). Human should confirm keep-rate vs omit.

**`electricpk`:**
- **kinetics[0].off** — Off is bi-exponential (2.09 ms fast + 69.07 ms slow, Fig. 5A) with component amplitudes NOT reported. Stored 18 ms uses a figure-estimated ~75:25 fast:slow split.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The rulebook default (50:50 → 35.6 ms) is scientifically wrong here (Fig. 5A shows the recovery is fast-dominated with no visible slow tail; 35.6 ms would contradict the paper's 'off 11× faster than Mermaid' claim). But the exact amplitude split cannot be read precisely off the noisy figure, so the stored value carries genuine uncertainty (plausibly ~10–25 ms). Kept 18 with a documented rationale rather than swap to a demonstrably-wrong 50:50 value or an equally-arbitrary alternative.
- **brightnessData[0]** — ratio 0.32 vs EGFP is a computed estimate from cpEGFP (GCaMP3) EC×QY ≈ 10741 / 33600; the ElectricPk paper reports no brightness/EC/QY, so the entry has source 'estimated from cpEGFP (GCaMP3) EC×QY vs EGFP' (not a DOI) and NO sourceFigure.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Judgment call: it is the only brightness datum on the page and is a legitimate curator-B2 component-FP fallback, but it has no paper location to put in sourceFigure. Deleting it removes all brightness data; inventing a figure label would be fabrication. Left as-is for the human to decide (keep / remove / re-source from a GCaMP3 paper such as Tian 2009).

**`flicr1`:**
- **brightnessData[0] (ratio 1458 vs arch)** — Molecular-brightness ratio 1458× vs arch exceeds the rulebook's 50–500× normal-vs-arch band; cited to Piatkevich 2020 (acschemneuro.0c00046) Table 1.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The Piatkevich 2020 paper is not in Papers/ and the publisher page could not be fetched in this environment (curl blocked, no WebFetch). Could not independently verify or correct the value; would need the source opened. Left as stored (proofread:false).
- **kinetics (Milosevic 2020 Fast TAU 11.8 ms, Fig. 2E)** — Milosevic reports a FlicR1 optical Fast TAU of 11.8±0.5 ms (500 Hz, RT) from a double-exponential fit, but only the fast component is given (slow τ and amplitudes not reported).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Storing the fast component alone violates rule 1b (would understate); the amplitude-weighted mean cannot be computed. Deliberately omitted rather than guessed — recorded here for awareness.
- **photostabilityData[0] (67% at 100 mW/mm² / 1 min)** — 67% is derived from the paper's single-exp τ=150 s (exp(−60/150)=0.67), but the re-digitized Fig. 3I curve gives F(60 s)≈0.70.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ photostabilityData legitimately coexists with photobleach (rulebook 1i) and cites the paper's own τ; the ~3-point discrepancy is within reading error. Left the paper-derived value; flagged for the human in case they prefer the digitized 70%.

**`flicr2`:**
- **dynamicRangeData[1].deltaF (2P)** — Stored 24 is the -70→+30 (100-mV) chord read off Fig. 2D; the paper's explicitly quantified FlicR2 number is 19±11% for the -40→+30 (70-mV) window (Fig. 2E).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ 24 is the correct 100-mV window per rulebook 1d and is consistent with the digitized F-V; changing to 19 would store a 70-mV-window value. Judgment call on which to display — left as-is with the distinction documented in the note.
- **sensitivityData / photobleach / apWidthData / addgene** — All genuinely absent for FlicR2 across both papers (no per-AP number, not in the bleach panel, no single-AP waveform, plasmid distributed as a gift not via Addgene).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ No primary-source data exists to fill them; fabricating or borrowing from siblings would be invalid. Recorded as genuine gaps, not defects.

**`forces1`:**
- **photostabilityData[0].brightnessRemaining** — Stored 57% @6 min; the Fig. S1.2B green endpoint digitizes to F≈0.53 (=53%).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Within figure-reading tolerance and matches the memory-verified value; the paper's photostability metric is AUC-based (not a raw endpoint), so 57 is defensible. Flagged for the human to decide between 57 (as stored) and 53 (literal endpoint) rather than silently changing a plausible value.

**`harclight1`:**
- **kinetics[0].note** — Note says the kinetics were 'Measured at 1.2 kHz' but the paper's Table S6 caption states the neurons were 'imaged at 1 kHz' (the 1200 Hz figure is Supp Fig 4, the response-speed panel, not the Table S6 kinetics dataset).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The kinetics entry is proofread:true; Step 0 forbids modifying its value/content without an explicit human recheck. The stored on/off values (5.10/5.70 ms) are correct regardless — only the acquisition-rate phrasing in the note is off.
- **kinetics[0].temperature** — Stored as '25°C' but the paper does not explicitly state the neuron-electrophysiology recording temperature (only spectroscopy at 23±2°C is stated).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ proofread:true entry; value is a defensible room-temperature convention and there is no paper-stated number to replace it with.

**`hvi`:**
- **kinetics[0] (HVI-Cy3)** — Stores fast component 1.70/2.50 ms; HVI+ Table S2 now gives the full bi-exponential → amplitude-weighted 2.04/3.48 ms (re-source to doi:10.1126/sciadv.ads1807 Table S2).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Entry is proofread:true; the proofread rule forbids value replacement on locked entries. Requires human to unlock. This matches the prior audit's documented decision.
- **addgene** — No addgene field (optional).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ HVI is a dye-conjugated chemigenetic sensor; the deposited plasmid would be the Ace2(C81 mutant) scaffold, and no clear Addgene ID was located in the papers. Judgment call — left absent rather than guess.

**`hviplus`:**
- **addgene** — No Addgene entry; the paper reports no plasmid deposit and HVI+ uses Tz-dye bioorthogonal chemistry rather than a single deposited plasmid.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Genuine absence — nothing to add. Optional field per rulebook.
- **sensitivityData[0].deltaF** — Stored 22.3% (main-text/Fig. 2C headline) differs slightly from Table S2's 21.9% for the same HVI+-Cy3b per-AP measurement.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Both are the paper's own values (different n-set/rounding, not an error); the Fig. 2C headline value with its correct sourceFigure is the appropriate display value. Left as-is.

**`jarvis`:**
- **photobleach[0].t75 (extrapolated:true, 317 s)** — t75 lies 82 s beyond the last digitized point (235 s, where F=0.80); the biexp fit is extrapolated from 0.80 down to 0.75 with no paper-stated t75 anchor.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Rulebook leans to an honest lower bound (omit t75, note the bound) when no paper anchor exists, BUT the fit is clean (r²=0.99), the extrapolation is small, and it is already flagged extrapolated:true with an explanatory note. Reversing a deliberate prior decision is a judgment call for the human.
- **brightnessData (0.42×pace, 4.08×jedi2p)** — Both are SNR-derived proxies (F0∝(SNR/ΔF/F)²), not direct EC×QY or relative-fluorescence measurements; directionality is counterintuitive (dimmer than pAce yet 4× JEDI-2P).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Notes honestly disclose the proxy method, the math is internally consistent and reproduces the measured Jarvis/pAce ratio, and this is the page's established method (0.42×pace pre-existed). Keep-vs-remove of a proxy is an explicit judgment call reserved for the human.

**`jedi1p`:**
- **brightnessData[2] / researchPapers (Nair ACS 2025, Pan bioRxiv 2026)** — 1.71×ASAP3 (ACS Fig 2C) and the Pan-2026 fMRI paper values are stored but those PDFs are not in the local corpus, so they were not re-opened this pass.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Values are internally consistent and were verified in a prior audit; re-opening would require fetching paywalled/preprint sources not present locally. No evidence of error — left as-is rather than guess.
- **siblingId (jedi1p↔jedi2p)** — Reciprocal siblings sharing parent asap2s but published in different papers (JEDI-1P 2023 Nat Commun vs JEDI-2P 2022 Cell).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Strict sibling rule prefers same-paper, but the molecular relationship (both directly evolved from ASAP2s) is real and the prior curator chose to keep the Y-fork. Judgment call for the human, not an unambiguous error.

**`jedi3hyp`:**
- **researchPapers** — Only 1 paper (the 2026 origin). Independent-use papers not found.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Sensor is brand-new (2026); no qualifying independent papers exist yet. Not an error — flagged for periodic recheck as adoption grows.

**`jedi3sub`:**
- **researchPapers** — Only the origin paper (Nature Methods 2026) is listed; no independent-usage papers.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ A citation/keyword sweep needs web access not used in this audit; for a just-published 2026 sensor an exhaustive external search is low-yield and the origin paper is correctly present as first entry.
- **spectrum (inheritance choice)** — No JEDI-3sub-specific published 1P spectrum exists; I inherited jedi2p's real curve rather than keeping the ASAP4 cpGFP curve.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Flagging as a judgment call for visibility — it is the physically correct provenance (paper says 1P ~JEDI-2P, VSD-only mutations), but it is an inheritance decision, not a directly-measured JEDI-3sub curve.

**`lotusv`:**
- **dynamicRangeData** — 2019 paper Fig 1B reports a distinct neuronal ΔR/R0 of 5.3% over −120→+80 mV (V½ −45.5 mV) — smaller than the HEK293T 21% because amplitude changes by cell type  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ It is a 200-mV full-range value, not a clean −70→+30 (100-mV) step; storing it would violate the DR window rule (overstated window). Left out and documented; would need a figure-read of the −70→+30 chord off Fig 1B to store properly, which the noisy neuronal F-V may not support.
- **familyTreePath** — Leaf node uses familyTreePath rather than a parentId; rulebook prefers parentId for leaves  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ LOTUS-V was built de novo (NanoLuc+Venus into Ci-VSP VSD(R217Q)) with no single GEVI in src/gevis it was evolved from; assigning a parentId (e.g. a VSFP/Mermaid2) would be a fabricated lineage. Judgment call left for human.

**`macq-morange2`:**
- **apWidthData[0].fwhm** — 16 ms is low-confidence: the only mOrange2 single-AP source is the noisy single-trial spike train in Fig. 5a (no averaged waveform panel exists for mOrange2), and baseline noise inflates the half-max crossings. mCitrine's cleaner Fig. 5c waveform is ~11 ms and mOrange2's faster off-kinetics predict a narrower true optical AP, so 16 ms is probably an over-estimate.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Cannot do better than the honest reading from the available figure without fabricating precision. Kept the measured value with a caveat rather than the unsupported prior 8.5 ms; whether to keep-vs-remove an imprecise apWidth is a judgment call for the human.
- **crossGevi arclight apWidthData** — Arclight's optical-AP FWHM is plotted in Fig. 5c (blue) but my measurement (~21 ms) was contaminated by the blue 'Arclight' legend dot in adjacent panel d.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Out of scope (cannot edit arclight.json in this scoped pass) AND the reading is unreliable — would need a dedicated tight crop of just the blue waveform before adding it.

**`marina`:**
- **apWidthData** — No optical single-AP FWHM is stated anywhere in the paper, and the only single-AP-scale figure (Fig 5C) is a low-pass-filtered (Kaiser-Bessel, 150 Hz cutoff) burst of ~5-6 spikes at coarse ~1600 ms/panel resolution rather than a clean averaged single-AP optical waveform with a fine time axis.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Deriving an FWHM from a filtered, temporally-compressed multi-spike trace would be unreliable and would violate the no-fabrication rule. Genuine absence — no usable single-AP waveform and no stated width in this or any listed paper.
- **sensitivityData[0].sourceFigure** — The 1.4%/AP value and SNR 4.5 are stated in the running main-text paragraph; sourceFigure is set to 'Fig. 5' (the per-AP recording figure) rather than 'Main text'.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Judgment call — Fig. 5 is the actual data figure the value is derived from and is a defensible attribution; not clearly wrong, so left unchanged rather than guess.

**`mermaid`:**
- **apWidthData** — No optical single-AP FWHM is stated (only the electrical DRG-AP stimulus half-width 2.1 ms) and no clean single-AP optical waveform is plotted to digitize.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Genuine absence per rulebook 1j — the 2.1 ms is a voltage-command width, not an optical AP FWHM; fabricating one would violate the no-fabrication rule. Left absent (INFO).
- **kinetics[0]** — 33°C on/off (8/15) are single-component reads; whether a slow on-component persists at 33°C is not explicitly stated by the paper.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Fig. 1f plots no red τ_on(slow) at 33°C and the text frames 33°C kinetics as single 5-20 ms values; storing a weighted mean would require inventing a slow τ + amplitude. Value kept as the paper's framing; only the note was corrected.

**`mermaid2`:**
- **apWidthData** — Paper reports optical single-AP responses (Fig 6B) but only ELECTRICAL AP half-widths (1.85/1.50 ms, Suppl Fig 6) as numbers; no averaged single-AP optical waveform with a ms scale bar suitable for FWHM digitization.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Rulebook 1j requires an OPTICAL single-AP FWHM (stated or digitizable optical waveform). Fig 6B shows single-trial AP-train ratio responses, not a clean averaged single-AP optical trace; digitizing an FWHM from it would be unreliable/fabricated. Genuine absence, not fillable.
- **brightnessData/photostabilityData/photobleach/addgene** — None reported in the origin paper (the only paper).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ No EC×QY, no EGFP/relative-intensity comparison, no photobleach figure, no photostability value anywhere in main text or supplement; plasmid distributed via RIKEN BRC (not Addgene). Genuine absences.

**`nirbutterfly`:**
- **kinetics** — Song 2024 (Neurophotonics, Fig 3d) reports a population synaptic-response decay τ for nirButterfly (17.5 ± 3.15 ms single-pulse; 15.5 ± 1.11 ms 5-pulse, single-exp fit, acute brain slices, Schaffer-collateral stim).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ This is a population field-response decay in acute slices — a different prep and quantity than the stored HEK293 single-cell voltage-step τOFF (2.13 ms). Adding it as an off-value would be non-comparable and would pollute the displayed kinetics median. Judgment call left for the human.
- **dynamicRangeData[0]** — Table 1 lists −16%/100 mV while the stored primary DR is the neurons donor value −9.8% ΔF/F; the two disagree within the same paper.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The prior audit deliberately stored the explicitly-stated neurons donor −9.8% and documented the Table-1 −16% in the note; the −16 may be a best-case/full-range figure. Not a clear error to overwrite — left as-is per the documented decision.

**`novarch`:**
- **subthresholdData[0].slope** — slope 0.41 %/mV is derived from the whole-range 41%/100 mV, not a measured −90→−50 mV subthreshold slope (paper reports none).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The F-V is near-linear so the average slope ≈ subthreshold slope, and the note honestly states the derivation; removing vs keeping is a judgment call for the human. Not fabrication — flagged for review.
- **kinetics[0].temperature** — HEK293 voltage-clamp recording temperature (25°C) is an assumption, not stated in the paper.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The paper genuinely does not state the HEK recording temperature; the entry already discloses the assumption in its note. No source exists to correct it.

**`pace`:**
- **dynamicRangeData[2] (43.5%) and sensitivityData[3] (19.5%)** — Both cite doi:10.25378/janelia.21534411, which is a GENIE-team conference POSTER ('Optimization of genetically encoded voltage and calcium indicators for in vivo imaging', Nov 2022) about Ace2N-based/chemigenetic (Voltron/Positron) indicators — not the Kannan/Pieribone pAce sensor. A poster is a weak, non-peer-reviewed, effectively uncitable source; the 'Fig. 1'/'Fig. 2' labels are vague; and I could not confirm pAce appears on it. Values are redundant with better-sourced entries (DR: 30.5, 41; sensitivity: 10.7, 43, 19.9).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Rulebook says to remove unverifiable/vague-attribution entries, but I cannot open the poster to definitively prove pAce is absent, and deleting data the human may have deliberately added is a judgment call. Recommend removing both; deferring to human.
- **photostabilityData[1].duration** — Stored duration '~42 min' for the 10 mW/mm² Fig 1h HeLa photobleach recording is not stated in the mBaoJin Fig 1h legend (which only gives cell counts). Only the '80% loss = 20% remaining' figure is text-verified (line 230–231).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Could not read the Fig 1h x-axis endpoint from the local PDF (figure image is embedded/at end and did not render as text). The retained-% value (20%) is correct; only the duration is unverified. Not fabricating a replacement.

**`pacer`:**
- **dynamicRangeData[0] / kinetics[0]** — Both carry proofread:true and their values were independently re-verified as correct against Table S1 (kinetics 3.78/4.13 amplitude-weighted; DR 27.8%/100mV). Left proofread:true.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Values are correct and the rulebook forbids overriding a human proofread flag except to fix a genuine defect; there is no defect in these two.
- **researchPapers** — Could not run Semantic Scholar / PubMed citation-tracking to fully confirm the paper list is exhaustive.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ curl to api.semanticscholar.org is blocked in this sandbox and WebFetch is unavailable. Local greps of candidate PDFs (jarvis, voltron2) found only reference-list citations, not experimental use, so the 2-paper list is likely complete for a niche 2022 sensor — but not machine-verifiable here.
- **dynamicRangeData** — Kannan (27.8%) and Liu (14.7%) report substantially different pAceR ΔF/F per 100 mV in HEK.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Both are legitimate 1P published measurements from different labs; not an error to correct — both stored, discrepancy documented in notes.

**`positron`:**
- **dynamicRangeData[0]** — deltaF=23 labeled as the +30 mV / −70→+30 value, but Fig 2a reads ~20% at +30 mV and ~23% at +50 mV — the stored 23 appears to be the +50 mV read.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Entry is proofread:true (human-locked); rulebook forbids value replacement for proofread entries. The correct −70→+30 value (~20%) is already captured in the adjacent DR[1] entry.
- **researchPapers** — Janelia figshare (doi:10.25378/janelia.21534411), cited as the source for dynamicRangeData[3] and sensitivityData[1], is not listed in researchPapers.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ It is a figshare dataset deposit (PatcherBot assay), not a peer-reviewed research paper that used Positron experimentally; adding it to researchPapers is a judgment call the prior audit deliberately declined. Left for human decision.

**`positron2`:**
- **parentId** — Positron2 (R78K N81D D92N W178F) shares R78K+W178F with pAce (differ only S81D->N81D) but shares N81D+D92N with Positron (N81D D92N E199V). Whether it was engineered from pAce or from Positron is not stated in the figshare set.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Direct molecular parent not stated in the only available source; kept the existing pace assignment (plausible: positive-going scaffold + shared R78K/W178F) rather than guessing a change.
- **kinetics/brightnessData/photostabilityData/photobleach/apWidthData** — All absent for Positron2.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ No available source reports them (Positron2 is a 2022 figshare screening variant absent from the 2020 Positron paper and from HVI+/Voltron2/ASAP4 supplements); nothing to read or digitize.

**`quasar1`:**
- **photobleach** — Paper states QuasAr1 photobleaching monoexponential time constant τ=440 s at 300 W/cm² (main text), but no fluorescence-vs-time bleach FIGURE for QuasAr1 exists (Supp. Fig. 6D is an ArcLight AP train, not a QuasAr bleach curve).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Building a photobleach[] curve from a single stated τ (no digitizable figure) would be synthesizing/fabricating data points, which the rules forbid. The existing photostabilityData (87% @ 3000 mW/mm²/1 min, derived from τ=440 s) already captures this honestly. If desired, a monoexponential photobleach entry could be constructed analytically from τ=440 s (t75 = 440·ln(1/0.75) = 127 s at 3000 mW/mm²) — deferred as a judgment call.
- **researchPapers** — Only 1 paper (the origin, Hochbaum 2014).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ QuasAr1 was superseded by QuasAr2 within the same publication and is essentially unused independently in the literature (only quasar2.json cross-references it internally). A single-paper list is accurate, not a defect — left as-is per prior audit and to avoid attributing QuasAr2-only papers to QuasAr1.

**`quasar2`:**
- **brightnessData[1] (ratio 10, reference "arch")** — Monakhov 2020 Table 1's brightness value of 10 for QuasAr2 is normalized to Arch(D95N)=1 (footnote b), NOT wild-type Arch. It is stored as reference:"arch" (the WT Arch page), conflicting with entry[0] (3.4× vs WT Arch from Hochbaum). This injects a contradictory edge into the shared brightness graph.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Fixing requires either removing a real data point or re-pointing to an arch-d95n reference that does not exist in src/gevis/. Both are judgment calls affecting the cross-GEVI brightness graph; not a mechanical correction. Recommend removing entry[1] (entry[0] already captures WT-Arch) or creating an arch-d95n node.
- **sensitivityData[1] (ncomms5625, deltaF 48)** — Redundant ref-23 re-citation of the Hochbaum 48% per-AP value; not an independent measurement.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Kept (with a flag note) per the established arclight/asap1 precedent of retaining re-citations; whether to delete the duplicate outright is a human preference call. Display median is 48% either way.

**`quasar3`:**
- **apWidthData[0].fwhm** — 1.8 ms is a store-only value derived by prior manual digitization of Fig. 1i blue Soma STA (88 spikes). My independent high-dpi auto re-digitization was contaminated by adjacent legend text/panel j and gave an unreliable width.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Value is visually plausible (soma trace is clearly the narrowest peak vs dendrites; fast opsin at 1 kHz) and is store-only (not displayed). Overwriting with a worse automated number would degrade it. Left the documented manual value.

**`quasar6`:**
- **researchPapers (Haziza 2025 Cell)** — Added on strong evidence it evolved+applied QuasAr6a via Photopick, but SomArchon is its headline sensor and it is primarily a fiber-photometry/TEMPO paper — could not open full text to confirm QuasAr6a was used in an experiment vs mentioned.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Kept the entry (search strongly indicates QuasAr6a use) but flag for the human to confirm experimental use and, if only mentioned, remove it.
- **sensitivityData[0].deltaF** — Fig. 3i shows QuasAr6a per-spike ΔF/F ≈ 0.238 (~24%); stored value is 23.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Within visual-reading tolerance and the entry is proofread:true; a 1% change to a human-approved entry is not warranted.

**`quasar6b`:**
- **voltage.custom** — Purple QuasAr6b F-V trace in Ext. Fig. 4c appears mildly sublinear (shallow near −70 to −20 mV) whereas the stored curve is a straight ramp.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Paper explicitly reports a linear F-V from −70 to +30 mV with R=0.994 and the endpoint (24%) matches dynamicRangeData; entry is proofread:true. A linear representation is faithful to the paper's own characterization, so no re-digitization was warranted.

**`restus-ni`:**
- **kinetics** — τs,on, τs,off and slow fraction rs are strongly voltage-dependent (Fig. S6C); the single stored on/off necessarily fixes one reference voltage (chosen: 0 mV depolarizing step).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ No single 'correct' voltage exists for a store-one-value schema; I chose a representative central depolarization and documented rs/τf/τs and the voltage-dependence in the note. A human may prefer a different reference voltage, but any choice is a judgment call, not an error.
- **restus.brightnessData** — restus↔asap3 = 1.96 (this paper) disagrees with Table S1-derived 1.71.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Lives in another GEVI's file (restus.json); editing it is out of scope to avoid parallel-write clobber. Recorded in crossGeviTODO for the main agent.

**`restus`:**
- **researchPapers[3], researchPapers[4]** — Two 2026 application papers (Bernert et al., Mol Brain, KCNH crosstalk; Malan et al., Stem Cell Research, rEstus iPSC line) could not be independently re-verified this session — WebFetch tool was unavailable, so I could not confirm they experimentally used rEstus.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ They carry specific DOIs + descriptive samples from a prior curator pass and are plausible rEstus application/line papers; removing them on an unverified basis would be worse than leaving them. Flagged for the human to spot-check.
- **subthresholdData[0].note** — Note states sibling rEstus2s 'reaches ~1.8 %/mV'; Fig. S2 shows the red rEstus2s curve peaking closer to ~2.0 %/mV.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ It is a note-only descriptive aside about the SIBLING, not rEstus's own value (which is correct at 1.25 %/mV, verified). Editing a sibling-descriptive string carries more risk of introducing an inconsistency than the minor imprecision it fixes; left for human discretion.

**`solaris`:**
- **parentId** — parentId is 'cepheid1b' but the paper says Solaris replaced the FP in Cepheid's ECL1 with cpHaloTag and refers to 'Cepheid1b/s' generically (both cepheid1b and cepheid1s exist as pages).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Judgment call on which Cepheid variant is the true molecular parent; cepheid1b (brightness variant) is defensible, but the paper does not name a single specific parent. Left for human decision.
- **sensitivityData[0].note (JF585)** — Note states '-28.1 ± 1.3%' while Fig. 2B prints '-28.1 ± 1.2%' (the ±1.3 matches the abstract).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ deltaF value (-28.1) is correct and both error bars trace to the same measurement; a ±0.1% error-bar wording discrepancy is cosmetic, not a data error.

**`somarchon`:**
- **dynamicRangeData[0].deltaF** — Stored 30%; Fig.1g −70→+30 step reads ~28% and Monakhov Table 1 lists SomArchon at 28%/100mV. 30 is ~2% high.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Entry is proofread:true — rulebook forbids value replacement on proofread entries; only the human may unlock/change it.
- **photobleach (cross-GEVI, asap3 & voltron)** — ED Fig.5e co-plots ASAP3-Kv and Soma-Voltron525; rulebook 1i mandates cross-GEVI overlay entries, but the co-plotted sensors were imaged at 475/488 nm ~25 mW/mm² vs SomArchon 637 nm/1500 mW/mm² (~60× intensity gap, different wavelength) so an overlay is not an apples-to-apples comparison.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Cross-GEVI writes are out of scope (parallel-clobber rule) AND the overlay is scientifically questionable — recorded in crossGeviTODO with a recommendation against adding, for the main agent/human to adjudicate.
- **sensitivityData[2].deltaF (striatum)** — Stored 28%; Fig.1d striatum box-plot median reads ~29-30%.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Within box-plot digitization tolerance (±2%); changing it would add fabricated precision to a proofread:false read without clear benefit.

**`varnam`:**
- **dynamicRangeData[2].deltaF** — −17.9% is over a 110-mV (−70→+40) window, not the 100-mV standard; the true −70→+30 value is smaller but could not be read reliably off the crowded overlapping Fig 1B curves.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Reading a −70→+30 point off overlapping curves would be fabrication/estimation; kept the paper's explicit 110-mV number with the window honestly documented in the note (no extrapolation).
- **parentId** — parentId='ace2n-mneon' but VARNAM's true direct molecular parent is Ace-mRuby3 (Ace-WR-mRuby3 N81S).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ No ace-mruby3 page exists in src/gevis/; ace2n-mneon is the nearest existing predecessor (same Ace opsin + Opsin-FRET architecture). Changing it would break the visible lineage without a better target — a judgment call for the human.
- **dynamicRangeData[0] / sensitivityData[1] / subthresholdData** — Beck 2019 (Sci Rep) DR −10.8 (Fig 2c), sens 5.3 (Fig 3a), and Abdelfattah 2023 (Neuron) subthreshold 0.104 %/mV (Fig S5E) not independently re-verified.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Those PDFs are not in the local Papers directory; values are plausible and pre-existing, so left unchanged rather than removed.

**`varnam2`:**
- **kinetics[0].proofread, dynamicRangeData[0].proofread, brightnessData[0].proofread** — Three entries carry proofread:true that was set by a prior agent, not the human (rulebook: only the human sets true).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ All three re-verified correct against Table S1 / Fig S11A this pass, so no value is at risk. Demoting to false is a compliance judgment call for the human — the rule tells me never to SET true, not to demote existing true flags — so I left them and flagged it rather than guessing.

**`vega`:**
- **parentId** — parentId = cepheid1b, but Vega is engineered from the Cepheid *design template* (Ace2(D81C)+FP-in-loop) not from a specific Cepheid variant; cepheid1b vs cepheid1s is ambiguous and neither variant's DR matches the paper's quoted Cepheid −33.6%.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Lineage judgment call; no evidence in the paper singles out one Cepheid variant as the molecular parent, and changing it is not clearly warranted. Left as-is (design-template rationale, consistent with prior audit).

**`voltron`:**
- **researchPapers** — Voltron2 (Neuron 2023), SomArchon (Nature 2019), Solaris (bioRxiv 2024), Positron (Nat Commun 2020) supply cross-GEVI Voltron data via source DOIs but are not listed in researchPapers.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ These are sibling/development papers whose primary subject is a different GEVI, not Voltron-application papers. The page convention lists application papers only. Adding them is a judgment call for the human.
- **photobleach[0].illumination / photostabilityData[1].illumination (intensity)** — The SomArchon Ext. Fig. 5e caption states 510/25 nm excitation but does not restate the intensity specifically for the photobleach panel e; 25 mW/mm2 is inferred from the co-panel and the original Voltron imaging range (25-29 mW/mm2).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Intensity is a reasonable inference and matches multiple sources; only the wavelength was demonstrably wrong (fixed). Not changing the 25 mW/mm2 value without a panel-e-specific statement.

**`voltron2`:**
- **dynamicRangeData / sensitivityData (cross-paper entries)** — The HVI+ (Table S2), Vega (Fig 1C), Solaris (Fig 1C/2B), and Janelia patch-assay entries were not re-opened against their own source papers this pass (only the primary Neuron 2023 paper was deep-verified).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Each carries a detailed provenance note and proofread:false; re-verifying 4 additional papers is out of scope for a primary-paper-focused pass and none showed internal inconsistency. Flag for a future cross-paper verification if desired.

**`vsfp1`:**
- **researchPapers** — Only 1 paper (Sakai 2001). Could not re-run the Semantic Scholar citation crawl (api.semanticscholar.org blocked by the sandbox in this session).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ VSFP1 the prototypic construct was poorly-trafficked and immediately superseded by VSFP2.x, so experimental re-use in later papers is not expected; the prior audit ran the crawl and confirmed exhaustive. Left as-is rather than fabricate papers; flagged for the human if a fresh crawl is wanted.
- **voltage.custom** — F-V points lie on the paper's stated linear regression line (re-referenced −70=0) rather than being raw scatter markers read at absolute voltages.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Fig. 3B's x-axis is ΔVm (change), not absolute Vm, so scatter markers cannot be uniquely mapped to absolute membrane potentials; the paper characterizes the F-V ONLY as a linear slope (1.8±0.1%/100mV). Hand-read scatter gave noisier/steeper chords. Retaining the authoritative linear slope (trimmed to measured span) is the honest representation for a purely-linear sensor — a judgment call worth human awareness.

**`vsfp2_3`:**
- **kinetics (Mishina 2014 entry)** — Recording temperature not restated in Mishina 2014 Table 1 for the VSFP2.3* row (values sourced 'From Mishina et al. 2012'). Stored as 35°C.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ 35°C is the Knöpfel-lab PC12 patch-fluorometry standard (used in Mutoh 2009 & Lundby 2010, both local) but is not printed in this table; the definitive value would be in Mishina 2012 Neuron, which is not available locally. Noted transparently in the entry rather than fabricating certainty.
- **voltage.custom (F-V)** — F-V is a normalized-Boltzmann shape (Fig. 3B) rescaled to ΔR/R via a 15.2% full-range anchor, not signed ΔF/F points read directly from a figure.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The paper only publishes a normalized (0→1) acceptor ΔF-V curve — no signed ΔF/F-vs-V figure exists — so direct reading is impossible. The rescale follows the project's documented [[fv-normalized-boltzmann-rescale]] method and is already fully disclosed in voltage.note; changing it would require a signed-ΔF/F figure that does not exist.

**`vsfp2`:**
- **parentId** — parentId 'vsfp1' is a conceptual successor, not a direct molecular parent — VSFP1 is Kv2.1-VSD CFP/YFP FRET whereas VSFP2.1 is a Ci-VSP-VSD redesign (R217Q); the two share no direct engineering lineage beyond the VSFP branding.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ No nearer parent exists in src/gevis/ (VSFP2A–D have no pages; only vsfp1, vsfp2, vsfp2_3, vsfp3_1 exist). The VSFP-series tree convention keeps vsfp1 as the branch anchor. Changing it would orphan the node. Judgment call for the human — left as-is per prior audit.
- **researchPapers** — Only 2 papers; not re-swept via Semantic Scholar/PubMed this pass.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ VSFP2.1 was a 2007 prototype immediately superseded by VSFP2.3; independent downstream voltage-imaging use of the 2.1 variant specifically is effectively nil, and the 2 listed Knöpfel-lab characterization papers are appropriate. A full citation sweep is unlikely to add qualifying entries; prior audit reached the same conclusion. Flag for the human if a broader sweep is desired.

**`vsfp3_1`:**
- **kinetics.off** — Deactivation time constant (τ_off) for VSFP3.1_Cerulean is not present in any accessible source — it is only in Perron ChemBiol Table S1 (supplementary), which is not in the local PDF.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ No primary source available to read the value; fabricating or estimating it is disallowed. Genuine data gap.

**`vsfpbutterfly`:**
- **brightnessData[0].sourceFigure** — Missing sourceFigure; the entry is a component-FP EC×QY estimate (mCitrine donor 77000×0.76 / 33600 EGFP anchor = 1.742 ≈ 1.74), not read from a paper figure.  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ The number is verified correct with the site-standard /33600 anchor, but there is no figure to cite. Adding a fabricated figure label would be wrong; removing a valid FRET component-FP proxy is a judgment call. Left as-is per prior audit convention; flagged for human decision.
- **voltage.sourceFigure vs custom values** — sourceImage/sourceFigure is Fig. 2C (normalized Boltzmann) while the signed deltaF amplitudes are effectively supported by Fig. 2B (ΔR/R0 step traces).  
  _why not auto-fixed:_ Both panels describe the same PC12 F-V and the digitized shape does come from the Fig. 2C Boltzmann (rescaled to 15%); the values were cross-checked against Fig. 2B and agree. No mismatch to correct — noted for transparency.

## QA Findings (flagged edits)

**`archer2`** — One issue requires a fix. The '60% ΔF/F per 100 mV' addition is correct and well-sourced. However, '99× vs Arch WT' is inaccurate as written: Flytzanis 2014 reports two values — ×55 (peak current) and ×99 (steady-state current) — consistently presented as a range '55–99×' in the abstract and as paired figures in the Results. The description cherry-picks only the larger number. The agent should revert or amend the description to say '55–99×' (or at minimum '99× steady-state / 55× peak').
- ❌ **description: '99× vs Arch WT' photocurrent suppression** (high conf): Flytzanis 2014 reports TWO photocurrent-suppression figures for Archer2: ×55 (peak current) and ×99 (steady-state current) vs Arch WT, presented as a range '55–99 times' in the abstract and as paired values '×55 and ×99' in the Results (p.2). The description uses only '99×' with no qualification, silently dropping the ×55 peak figure and implying a single clean value. This is cherry-picking; the description should say '55–99×' or specify which measurement '99×' refers to (steady-state).

**`archon2`** — The JSON is valid and parses correctly. However, 3 of the 6 changes are WRONG and must be reverted: the editing agent made a severe ~28 nm error in re-digitizing the Archon1 emission spectrum from Song 2024 Fig. 1b. The peak is at ~698-699 nm in the actual figure (confirmed by three independent pixel analyses of the 300 dpi render), not at 670 nm as claimed. The prior peakEm=693 nm and the old 121-pt emission array were substantially more accurate. Changes 1, 3, and 4 (peakEm, custom emission array, and spectrum note) must all be reverted to the pre-edit state. Changes 5 and 6 (samplingRate 3200→2300 and the apWidthData note) are correct and should be kept — the Piatkevich 2018 Supplementary Figure 16 caption explicitly states 2.3 kHz for panels b/d/e/f/g, overriding the 3.2 kHz header that applied only to panel a. Change 2 (minEm 650→651) is a trivial 1-nm shift that is plausible but moot since the whole emission block needs reversion.
- ❌ **spectrum.peakEm 693→670** (high conf): WRONG. Independent pixel analysis of Song 2024 Fig. 1b (Archon1 emission, rendered at 300 dpi from /Users/dzgong/Documents/GEVIBench/Papers/archon1/024201_1.pdf page 2) shows the emission peak at approximately 698-699 nm — NOT 670 nm. The x-axis spans 650-770 nm with the axis running from pixel col 527 to col 1184 (657 px for 120 nm → 0.183 nm/px). The topmost true-black curve pixels (R<50, G<50, B<50, balanced channels to exclude the journal blue banner) reach minimum row 271 at columns 790-798, corresponding to 698-699 nm. The prior stored value of 693 nm was within ~5 nm of the true figure value; the new value of 670 nm is ~28 nm too blue. The editing agent likely miscalibrated the x-axis, perhaps mistaking the 670 nm tick for the peak column. The emission array must be reverted to its previous form (peak near 693 nm) and peakEm reverted to 693.
- ❌ **spectrum.custom.emission (121-pt→120-pt, peak at index 44→index 19)** (high conf): WRONG. The new 120-pt emission array peaks at index 19 (= wavelength 670 nm from minEm=651), which is ~28 nm too blue versus the actual Song 2024 Fig. 1b peak at ~698-699 nm (index ~47-48 from 651 nm). Three independent pixel analyses of the rendered figure all converge on ~698-699 nm peak. The old 121-pt array (peak at index 44 → 694 nm from minEm=650) was substantially closer to the true figure value. This change must be reverted.
- ❌ **spectrum.note rewrite (mentions peak ~670 nm, prior digitization ~693 nm mis-calibrated)** (high conf): WRONG in its factual claims. The note states the Song 2024 Fig. 1b peak is ~670 nm and that the prior ~693 nm was a mis-calibration. This is backwards: the actual figure peak is ~698-699 nm and the prior 693 nm was the more accurate digitization. The note must be reverted or corrected to reflect the actual ~693-699 nm peak range, removing the incorrect ~670 nm claim and the incorrect assertion that the prior 693 nm was wrong.

**`asap3`** — The new apWidthData entry is largely correct and well-supported by the primary source (Villette 2019 Cell Fig. 2F). One factual error: temperature is stored as '25°C' but the paper specifies room temperature = 23°C (HEK293A Methods section; neuron recordings follow 'as above'). The FWHM value of 12.7ms is approximately correct (my digitization gives 12.4ms for bar mean, 12.6ms for error cap) — within bar-chart reading tolerance and no revert needed. ACTION REQUIRED: change temperature '25°C' → '23°C' (or 'room temperature') in apWidthData[1].
- ❌ **apWidthData[1].temperature = '25°C'** (high conf): The Villette 2019 paper does NOT specify 25°C for the cultured hippocampal neuron AP recordings. The main text says 'at room temperature' (line 803) and the figure caption for panel E (same neuron preparation) does not state a temperature. The Methods section for 'Whole Cell Patch-Clamping and Imaging of Cultured Hippocampal Neurons' says recordings were 'performed as above with HEK293A cells', and the HEK293A section specifies 'room temperature (23°C)'. The correct temperature label should be '23°C' or simply 'room temperature'. The stored '25°C' is factually incorrect and should be corrected to '23°C' (or 'room temperature').

**`cepheid1b`** — The JSON is valid and all metadata fields (samplingRate 484, sample, modality 1P, source, sourceFigure) are correctly supported by the primary source. The apWidthData field was legitimately derived from Fig. 1D (no numeric FWHM given in the paper; plot-derived is appropriate). However, the FWHM value of 8.4 ms appears to be a moderate overestimate. My independent pixel-level digitization of Fig. 1D from the PDF (scale bar = 43 px = 20 ms) consistently gives ~7.0 ms (15 px) using both center-line and topmost-pixel methods with the peak region not clipped. The physical-consistency check (τoff = 4.99 ms → expected FWHM ≈ 7.2 ms) also puts the correct value closer to 7 ms. The agent's 8.4 ms is not grossly wrong (within the ±1.5 ms digitization uncertainty range of 6–9 ms), but the most likely correct value is ~7.0 ms. Recommend: revise fwhm from 8.4 to 7.0 (or ~7, acknowledging ~±1 ms uncertainty).
- ❌ **apWidthData[0].fwhm = 8.4 ms (Fig. 1D, 20-ms scale bar calibration)** (medium conf): The figure and method are valid — Fig. 1D does show a Cepheid1b averaged optical AP waveform with a 20-ms scale bar and no numeric FWHM stated in the text. The 484 Hz sampling rate and 'cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro' sample are both confirmed from the Methods. However, my independent digitization of the same figure (PDF render, maroon trace center-line, scale bar = 43 px = 20 ms) gives a consistent FWHM of ~7.0 ms (15 px / 43 px × 20 ms), not 8.4 ms. The agent measured 8.0 px at 0.95 px/ms (19 px scale bar, lower-res PMC JPEG). Translating the agent's 8.0 px to my image scale gives ~18 px, but I measure 15 px. The ~20% overestimate (8.4 vs 7.0 ms) is consistent with using the lower edge rather than the center of the thick trace for the half-max calculation, or with a ~2 px error per crossing side. Physical consistency check: kinetics τoff = 4.99 ms → expected FWHM ≈ 7.2 ms; agent's 8.4 ms implies τoff ≈ 5.8 ms. Recommended value: 7.0 ms (±0.5 ms). The 8.4 ms should be revised downward.

**`electraoff`** — 6 of 7 changes confirmed correct. The photostability re-digitization (both S4h and Fig 1h), modality tags, and sourceFigure correction are all valid. The one failure is apWidthData[0].fwhm=7.5ms: independent pixel measurement from Fig 1j gives ~5.0ms (scale bar 101px=50ms, FWHM 10px), a ~50% discrepancy that cannot be explained by image resolution differences. The agent appears to have used an incorrect scale-bar calibration (126px/50ms instead of the actual ~101px/50ms at ~400dpi). Action required: re-verify FWHM from Fig 1j with careful scale-bar measurement, likely revert to ~5.0ms.
- ❌ **apWidthData[0] new entry: fwhm=7.5, samplingRate=1000, sample='Mouse, primary hippocampal neurons, in vitro', modality='1P', temperature='22°C', sourceFigure='Fig. 1j'** (medium conf): fwhm=7.5ms is NOT confirmed. My independent pixel measurement from the ElectraOFF panel in Fig 1j (bottom-right quadrant): scale bar = 101px = 50ms at ~400dpi (2.02px/ms); spike peak y=675, baseline y≈727, half-max y≈701; FWHM ≈ 10px = 4.96ms. Converting to agent's 500dpi scale: 10×(126/101)=12.5px, still corresponding to ~5ms — nowhere near the agent's 19px=7.5ms. The ~50% discrepancy (5ms vs 7.5ms) cannot be explained by resolution differences. The metadata fields (temperature 22°C from Methods 'room temperature (22℃)', sample 'primary hippocampal neurons') are correct, and adding apWidthData from Fig 1j is conceptually valid, but the stored fwhm value should be re-verified. My reading is ~5ms; recommend reverting fwhm to 5.0 or re-measuring with a careful scale-bar calibration from the actual panel.

**`electraon`** — 2 of 3 substantive changes are confirmed correct (photostability duration 40 min and brightnessRemaining 100); 1 is wrong (apWidthData FWHM 2.5 ms). The agent likely rendered the PDF at 720 dpi internally while reporting '400 dpi', causing a 1.78× error in the scale-bar pixel count (160 vs 90 px per 50 ms). The correct FWHM from 400 dpi is ~5.4 ms. Action required: revert apWidthData[0].fwhm from 2.5 to either (a) a re-measured value of ~5–6 ms with an honest note, or (b) remove the field if confidence is too low. The JSON is valid and all other fields are intact.
