## Photobleach / Photostability Assessment
- photobleach[0]: mUKG donor, Suppl Fig 2a arc-lamp panel (455DF30, 1.23 W/cm² = 12.3 mW/mm²). I rendered Fig S2a at 600 dpi and read the legend leader lines: UKG→upper green (~0.26 end), Cerulean→upper cyan (~0.19), mUKG→**lower green** (~0.05 end), mMiCy→lower cyan (~0.04). The stored digitization correctly traces the lower green (mUKG). Curve monotonic non-increasing, fractions in (0,1]; t75=33 s sits in the smooth decay (not a transient) → t75 (not t50) correct; F=0.75 crossing interpolates to ~35 s ≈ stored 33 s ✓. Biexp fit (a=0.932, τ1=107.5, τ2=1267.4 s) non-degenerate (τ2 ≪ 1e6). modality 1P, intensityMWmm2=12.3 (1P, valid). sourceImage exists and matches. No change.
- photostabilityData illumination 12.3 mW/mm² re-confirmed from Suppl Methods ("cyan~green FPs … 1.23 W/cm²"). Both coexist (additive) per rulebook. No change.

## apWidth Assessment
- GENUINELY ABSENT. The only width in the paper is the DRG-neuron **electrical** AP command half-width = 2.1 ms (Suppl Methods) — that is the voltage-clamp stimulus waveform, not an optical FWHM. Main text: optical ratio responses were "still broad compared to the action potential waveforms." Fig. 1g plots spike-train responses (broad, not a clean averaged single-AP optical waveform with a ms scale bar). No stated optical FWHM and nothing cleanly digitizable → no apWidthData (INFO, no action).

Value is the paper's own 33°C framing ("5–20 ms for both"). Not flagged for the user beyond this note.

**QA (✅ all checks pass, jsonValid=True):** Both edits are correct and well-supported by the primary source. No reverts needed. The JSON remains valid. The sensitivityData note correction (per-AP value is single-channel YFP ΔF/F, not ratiometric ΔR/R) is directly backed by the paper's text. Lines 311-314: 'maximum steady-state ratiometric response … reached 53.5 ± 2.8% (n = 6 cells); For a 100 mV depolarization from −70 mV to +30 mV, ΔR/R was 48.5 ± 3.5%.' Lines 364-365 (Fig. 4B caption): 'Vmid = −32 mV, the effective charge valence (Zd) = 1.05.' Temperature ~33°C confirmed line 308. Old note said '~55% at strong depolarization' — paper's actual max is 53.5 ± 2.8%, so the correction removing the overstatement is warranted.

## GEVI Page Check: Mermaid2
File: src/gevis/mermaid2.json

## Issues Found
- [ ] sensitivityData[0] note mischaracterizes the metric as "ratiometric ΔR (YFP/CFP) per action potential" — WARNING. The paper (main text, p.4433) states the per-AP value (2.57 ± 0.54%) is "the fractional fluorescence change (ΔF) in the acceptor channel", a single-channel YFP ΔF/F, not ratiometric ΔR. The authors analysed the YFP channel alone for spike detection because the CFP channel is too dim. FIXED.
- [ ] dynamicRangeData[0] note said the curve "saturates near +55%" — INFO/minor overstatement. Figure max ≈53% and text max steady-state = 53.5 ± 2.8%. Tightened to paper's exact figures. FIXED.
- No CRITICAL issues. No fabricated spectrum, no fabricated F-V, no legacy score fields, all entries carry proofread:false, all sourceFigure formats conform.

Acceptor = "YFP" (not further named in the paper). Stored composite excitation = FPbase ECFP, emission = FPbase EYFP, peaks 435/529. ## Cross-GEVI Updates
- None. The paper compares Mermaid2 only against its own predecessors Mermaid and Mermaid2β qualitatively (granule formation, Fig 3) and against ArcLight-A242 qualitatively (spike-train resolvability, Suppl Fig 5) — no quantitative kinetics/ΔF/brightness/FWHM values for other GEVIs to port. Mermaid2β (mUKG/mKOκ variant) has no page in src/gevis.

Fig 6B optical traces are single-trial AP-train responses, not an averaged single-AP optical waveform with a scale bar, so no reliable FWHM digitization is possible. Left absent.

- **kinetics** — Song 2024 (Neurophotonics, Fig 3d) reports a population synaptic-response decay τ for nirButterfly (17.5 ± 3.15 ms single-pulse; 15.5 ± 1.11 ms 5-pulse, single-exp fit, acute brain slices, Schaffer-collateral stim). _(why not fixed: This is a population field-response decay in acute slices — a different prep and quantity than the stored HEK293 single-cell voltage-step τOFF (2.13 ms). Adding it as an off-value would be non-comparable and would pollute the displayed kinetics median. Judgment call left for the human.)_
- **dynamicRangeData[0]** — Table 1 lists −16%/100 mV while the stored primary DR is the neurons donor value −9.8% ΔF/F; the two disagree within the same paper. _(why not fixed: The prior audit deliberately stored the explicitly-stated neurons donor −9.8% and documented the Table-1 −16% in the note; the −16 may be a best-case/full-range figure. Not a clear error to overwrite — left as-is per the documented decision.)_

**QA (⚠️ 3/4 checks flagged, jsonValid=True):** Both applied changes should be REVERTED. The agent correctly identified that the biexp fit gives F(555)=0.519 but then contradicted itself by claiming the curve 'crosses 0.5 at ~555 s' — 0.519 is above 0.5, not a crossing. The figure (SI Fig. S2) shows the blue curve reaching 0.5 only at the very end of the ~640 s window. The digitized data ends at F=0.503 at t=638 s (never dips below 0.5 within the recording). The biexp model t50 is ~660 s, beyond the window. The original notes — 'Half-decay (F→0.5) occurs near the end of the ~640 s recording' and 'the donor signal falls to ~0.50 by ~640 s (t50 ≈ end of window)' — were accurate descriptions of the figure and should be restored.
- ❌ photostabilityData[0].note — claim that blue curve crosses 0.5 at ~555 s (high): INCORRECT. Visual inspection of the rendered SI Fig. S2 (figs2c.png, full axis) shows the blue nirButterfly curve is still clearly above the dashed 0.5 line at ~550–555 s. It converges to 0.5 only at the very end of the ~640 s recording. The digitized data confirms: F(550)=0.520, F(572)=0.516 — still well above 0.5. The biexp fit gives F(555)=0.519. The true t50 from the biexp model is ~660 s (beyond the recording window). The ORIGINAL note — 'Half-decay (F→0.5) occurs near the end of the ~640 s recording' — was accurate and should be restored. The edit worsened accuracy by asserting a specific crossing time of ~555 s that is not supported by the figure or the data.
- ❌ photostabilityData[0].note — claim that curve 'ends at ~0.49 by ~640 s' (high): INCORRECT. The digitized fluorescence array ends at t=638 s with F=0.503, not 0.49. The biexp fit gives F(640)=0.503. The curve ends at ~0.503 (just barely at 0.5), not below 0.5. The original note was more accurate (implied value near 0.5 at end of window without claiming a mid-window crossing).
- ❌ photobleach[0].note — claim that donor reaches half-decay at ~555 s and ends at ~0.49 (high): INCORRECT for the same reasons. The biexp fit value at t=555 s is F=0.519, not 0.50. The biexp model's t50 is ~660 s (extrapolated beyond the window). F at t=638 s is 0.503 (not 0.49). The agent's cited evidence — 'biexp fit F(555)=0.519' — actually contradicts the claim that the curve crosses 0.5 at 555 s (0.519 > 0.5). The prior note 'the donor signal falls to ~0.50 by ~640 s (t50 ≈ end of window)' was accurate and should be restored.
- ✅ JSON validity (nirbutterfly.json parses as valid JSON) (high): File reads and parses correctly. All fields present, arrays well-formed, no syntax errors detected.

## Spectrum Check
- Status: Real (FPbase miRFP670 donor, verbatim). Source "FPbase: miRFP670", sourceFigure "FPbase" present.
- Action taken: none. Verified stored curve peaks at ex 644 nm / em 670 nm (self-consistent with peakEx/peakEm). FPbase canonical exMax is 642 nm; the 2-nm offset is digitization noise and peakEx=644 correctly matches the displayed curve's own peak, so no change. Emission peak 670 matches FPbase exactly. Curve is asymmetric (real), 194 ex + 207 em points at 1 nm — not fabricated.
- Source: FPbase. Correct for a FRET NIR sensor read out on the donor channel (miRFP670 donor / miRFP720 acceptor; acceptor FRET response weak → donor-only practical readout).

## F-V Curve Check
- Status: Real (digitized from Fig. 3b normalized ΔF/F–V, NIR GEVIb red curve, N=15 cells).
- Action taken: none. voltage.custom = [−100,−80,−70,−60,−40,−20,0,20,40,60] → deltaF [3,1,0,−1,−4,−6,−7,−9,−10,−11]. Verified monotonic non-increasing, −70=0, positive below −70 / negative above (consistent with polarity "negative"). Confirmed it is a faithful negated rescale of the figure's normalized curve: with n(−70)≈0.20 and scale A≈15, the rescale reproduces all stored points to ±1 (e.g. −40→−3.9, −20→−6.0, +20→−9.75). The −70→+30 chord ≈ −9.5% agrees with the −9.8% donor ΔF/F headline. sourceImage opened — it is the correct continuous ΔF/F-vs-V curve (three variants a/b/c), matching sourceFigure "Fig. 3b (NIR GEVIb)". Not a bar chart.
- Design note: the figure plots normalized |ΔF/F| going UP with depolarization; the page stores donor ΔF/F going DOWN (negative-going) per the prior user decision (donor is the practically-imaged channel; Table 1 compares in donor ΔF/F). Documented in voltage.note. This is an intentional, documented representation, not an error.
- Source: doi:10.1021/acschemneuro.0c00046.

## Flags / Judgment Calls
- Song 2024 acute-slice population synaptic-response decay τ (nirButterfly 17.5 ms 1-pulse / 15.5 ms 5-pulse, Fig 3d, single-exp) was NOT added to `kinetics`. Rationale: it is a population field-response decay under Schaffer-collateral synaptic stimulation in acute slices — a fundamentally different prep and quantity than the HEK293 single-cell voltage-step τOFF (2.13 ms). Adding it would pollute the displayed kinetics median with a non-comparable value. Recommend leaving out; flagged for human review.
- Table 1 lists −16%/100 mV for nirButterfly while the neurons text gives donor −9.8% ΔF/F; the page stores −9.8 (explicitly-stated neurons donor value) as primary DR and documents the −16 in the note. Left as the prior audit decided (defensible; the −16 may be a best-case or full-range figure).

## GEVI Page Check: pAce
File: src/gevis/pace.json

The page had been curated across three prior passes (Kannan 2022 Science; Grimm 2026 Neuron; Zhang 2025 mBaoJin). Treated as un-audited: every scientific value was re-verified against the three local PDFs. The page is in very good shape — one factual note error fixed, two entries flagged for human decision.

## Issues Found
- [x] **sensitivityData[0] note misattributes the sample** — CRITICAL (data integrity). Stored note said "PPL1-γ2α'1 dopaminergic neurons in Drosophila (n=87 spikes)". Kannan 2022 main text (lines 415–420) states the 10.7 ± 1.4% (n=87 spikes) value was measured in "vasoactive intestinal peptide–expressing (VIP+) interneurons in primary visual cortex (V1)... (Fig. 1, H and I)" in awake mouse in vivo — NOT Drosophila PPL1. Fig 1I is correct; the sample was wrong. The sibling apWidthData[0] already correctly says "Mouse, V1 VIP+ interneurons". FIXED (note corrected; value 10.7% unchanged; proofread true→false).
- [x] **dynamicRangeData[2] / sensitivityData[3] source (Janelia figshare 21534411)** — WARNING. That DOI resolves to a GENIE-team conference POSTER ("Optimization of genetically encoded voltage and calcium indicators for in vivo imaging", Nov 2022) whose subject is Ace2N-based/chemigenetic (Voltron/Positron) indicators — not pAce, which is a Kannan/Pieribone (Yale) sensor. A poster is a weak, non-peer-reviewed, uncitable source, and the "Fig. 1"/"Fig. 2" labels are vague. Could not confirm pAce even appears on it. Flagged (not deleted — judgment call).
- [x] **photostabilityData[1] duration "~42 min"** — INFO. The mBaoJin Fig 1h legend ("Photobleaching curves of Ace-based GEVIs in HeLa cells", n=14 pAce) does not state a total duration; only the 80%-loss figure is text-verified. Duration unverified.

## Spectrum Check
- Status: **Real** (verbatim FPbase mNeonGreen). peakEx 506 / peakEm 517 confirmed (excitation array max at 350+155=505≈506 nm; emission max at 472+45=517 nm). Asymmetric emission with a long red tail (269 pts) — not a Gaussian fake.
- Source: "FPbase: mNeonGreen", sourceFigure "FPbase" — correct (pAce's reporter is mNeonGreen; Ace2 opsin is the dark FRET acceptor; no separately measured pAce curve is published).
- Action: none.

## F-V Curve Check
- Status: **Real** (Kannan Fig 1D). voltage [-150…+50], deltaF [-23,-19,-14,-8,0,5,14,20,25,31,36]. Positive polarity: negative below −70, 0 at −70, monotonically increasing above — physically valid, no mid-range peak. −70→+30 step = +31% agrees with dynamicRangeData[0] 30.5% and DR-sign positive.
- sourceImage /fv-sources/pace.jpg opened and confirmed to depict the pAce positive-going ΔF/F–V curve matching Fig 1D (not a bar chart).
- Action: none.

Changes 1–5 (addgene id fix, both proofread clearances, sensitivityData[0] Drosophila→mouse V1 VIP+ note correction, sensitivityData[1] sourceFigure Fig. 2E→Table S2) are all well-supported by the papers. One issue flagged: dynamicRangeData[1] (deltaF=14.7, n=5 from Table S2) cannot be independently confirmed — Table S2 is a separate Excel file not locally accessible, and the n=5 in the stored note conflicts with the Fig. 2E caption stating n=6 for pAceR HEK293T measurements. - ✅ addgene.id 195518→195534 (high): Confirmed via web search: Addgene 195518 = pAAV-Syn-Ace-mNeon2 (Ace-mNeon2 construct, wrong sensor); Addgene 195534 = pAAV-CAG-DIO-pAceR-Kv2.1PR (pAceR, correct). The stored url already pointed to /195534/, so id and url now agree. Change is correct.
- ✅ addgene.proofread true→false (high): Rulebook-mandated: proofread must be cleared after any edit to the entry. Change is correct.

The ΔF/F values per spike (mean ± SEM)... 3.2 ± 0.5% for pAceR (n = 48 spikes).' Fig. 1H shows VIP+ neuron fluorescence traces, Fig. 1I shows expanded spike waveforms — consistent with sourceFigure Fig. 1I. The old note (Drosophila PPL1 attribution) was factually wrong; the correction is accurate. The Drosophila experiments (Fig. 1E–G) report d′ values, not per-spike ΔF/F.
- ✅ sensitivityData[0].proofread true→false (high): Rulebook-mandated after factual correction to the note. Change is correct.
- ✅ sensitivityData[1].sourceFigure Fig. 2E→Table S2 (high): Verified against HVI+ paper (ads1807) Fig. 2 caption: 'Fig. 2E: Fluorescence-voltage response curves of HVI+-Cy3b, pAce, pAceR, and Positron525 (n = 6 cells for each) measured in HEK293T cells.' Fig. 2E is unambiguously the F-V curve panel, not per-AP sensitivity. The 6.6 ± 0.5% per-AP value is stated in the main text as '(Fig. 2, A to E, and table S2)'; Table S2 is titled 'Voltage sensitivities of HVI series and Voltron series sensors' (confirmed from supplement cover page). Change to Table S2 is correct for a per-AP sensitivity source.
- ❌ dynamicRangeData[1] added: deltaF=14.7, sourceFigure=Table S2, n=5 (low): The 14.7 ± 0.7% value cannot be independently verified. Table S2 is a separate Excel file not available in the local PDF supplement — the supplement PDF cover page states 'Table S2 (separate file)'. The value does not appear in the main text. The note claims n=5 cells, but Fig. 2E caption explicitly says 'n = 6 cells for each' for the pAceR F-V measurements in HEK293T cells. The n=5 vs n=6 discrepancy is unexplained and raises doubt that this value is correctly read from Table S2. Visual inspection of Fig. 2E suggests pAceR reaches approximately 15% at +30 mV relative to -70 mV baseline, which is loosely consistent with ~14.7%, but this is not verification. ## Issues Found
- [ ] **addgene.id = "195518" is a broken/mismatched reference** — 195518 is the Ace-mNeon2 construct (pAAV-Syn-Ace-mNeon2); the url points to 195534 (pAAV-CAG-DIO-pAceR-Kv2.1PR, the correct pAceR construct). Verified both IDs live on Addgene.org. — CRITICAL (structural; checked even though entry was proofread:true)
- [ ] **sensitivityData[0] note factually backwards** — the note claimed the 3.2 ± 0.5% (n=48 spikes) came from "PPL1-γ2α'1 dopaminergic neurons in Drosophila ... not the V1 VIP+ mouse traces." The paper's own text (lines 395–421) shows the opposite: "We expressed each of our four GEVIs in VIP+ interneurons in primary visual cortex (V1) ... The ΔF/F values per spike ... 3.2 ± 0.5% for pAceR (n = 48 spikes)." The fly experiments (Fig 1E–G, fig S13) reported d′ ~6.7, not this ΔF/F. Value (3.2%) and sourceFigure (Fig 1I) are correct; only the prose was wrong. — WARNING
- [ ] **sensitivityData[1] sourceFigure "Fig. 2E" wrong** — in the HVI+ paper Fig 2E is the fluorescence–voltage response curve, not the per-AP sensitivity. The 6.6 ± 0.5% (n=5) per-AP value is stated in the running text and tabulated in Table S2. — WARNING
- [ ] dynamicRangeData had only the origin 27.8%; the HVI+ paper independently measured pAceR at 14.7%/100 mV (HEK293T) — added as a cross-source entry. — INFO/enrichment

## GEVI Page Check: Pado
File: src/gevis/pado.json — Kang & Baker 2016, Sci Rep, doi:10.1038/srep23865

## Issues Found
- None. This is a clean re-audit. Every scientific value and structural field was re-verified against the primary source (main PDF pages rendered + supplement text) and found correct. No edits applied.

## Proofread Skip Summary
- Skipped because `proofread: true`: 0 entries
- Added missing `proofread: false`: 0 (all 5 checkable entries — spectrum, voltage, kinetics[0], dynamicRangeData[0], researchPapers[0] — already carry `proofread: false`)
- Force mode: yes (treated page as UN-audited; re-verified all values against the paper regardless of prior audit memory)

## Spectrum Check
- Status: Real (proxy) — verbatim FPbase EGFP curve used as a near-exact representation of Super Ecliptic pHluorin A227D (SE227D), Pado's fluorophore.
- Rationale re-verified vs paper: Methods state the FP is "Super Ecliptic pHluorin A227D (SE227D)…first reported in the GEVI ArcLight." SE pHluorin carries S65T → collapses to a single ~488 nm excitation peak, spectrally ≈ EGFP; no measured SEpH ex/em curve is published. peaks 488/511 match EGFP; the excitation array retains EGFP's ~400 nm shoulder. The paper's imaging filter cube (Semrock GFP-30LP-B: 472/30 ex, 495 dichroic, 496 LP em) is fully consistent with a GFP-class fluorophore emitting ~511 nm. This is the identical, previously cross-verified (corr 1.0, max diff 0.0) EGFP-proxy pattern used for the sibling ArcLight/Marina SEpH pages.
- source: "FPbase: EGFP", sourceFigure: "FPbase" — present and correct. proofread:false.
- Action taken: none needed.

## F-V Curve Check
- Status: Real (2 figure points + baseline). Re-digitization NOT required.
- Verified vs Fig. 2a (rendered p4 at 200 dpi, zoomed): the black voltage-command trace (bottom) alternates 30 mV, 130 mV, 30 mV, 130 mV, 30 mV, 130 mV — i.e. exactly TWO depolarizing step levels from the −70 mV holding potential: +30 mV (a 100-mV step) and +130 mV (a 200-mV step). The red ΔF/F (F) trace shows a small downward deflection at +30 and a larger downward deflection at +130 (the +130 confounded by an upward pH baseline jump from Hv current, matching main-text prose). No other step voltages exist, so the stored `voltage [-70,30,130] / deltaF [0,-5,-10]` is the complete, correct set. There are NO fabricated/interpolated points (the prior audit's removal of −20/+80 mV holds).
- Baseline: deltaF at −70 mV = 0 ✓. Monotonicity: [0,−5,−10] strictly non-increasing with depolarization, consistent with polarity "negative" ✓. −70→+30 step (−5%) agrees in sign & magnitude with dynamicRangeData (−5%) ✓.
- sourceImage /fv-sources/pado.jpg opened & confirmed: it IS the Fig. 2a F/I/V trace crop showing the 30/130 mV steps (matches sourceFigure "Fig. 2a") — not a bar chart. source "doi:10.1038/srep23865" ✓.
- Action taken: none needed.

- public/photobleach-sources/positron.jpg — rendered/read HVI+ Fig S7 bleach curves; Positron525(orange)/Voltron2525(yellow) cross ~0.5 at ~205s, ~0.32 at 330s
- HVI+ paper main text (Papers/hviplus/main.txt) — Positron525 5.9±0.8% ΔF/F0 per AP (l.58,162); photobleach half-life 207±47s (l.114); Fig S5 F-V and per-AP (l.191)
- HVI+ supplement (Papers/hviplus/sm.txt) — Table S5 Positron525 t½ 207±47s n=18 at 532nm 5.0 W/cm² (l.202-212); Fig S5A F-V normalized to −70mV, Fig S5B per-AP 484 Hz (l.55-70); Fig S6/Table S4 RFWHM is HVI/HVI+ only, ratio metric (l.75-79,176)
- HVI+ Table S2 xlsx (Papers/hviplus/ads1807_table_s2.xlsx) — Positron525 kinetics depol τfast1.31(86.4%)/τslow74.54(13.6%), hyperpol τfast1.42(86.3%)/τslow69.56(13.7%) → weighted 11.27/10.76 confirmed exact

## GEVI Page Check: Positron
File: src/gevis/positron.json — Abdelfattah et al., *Nat. Commun.* 2020 (doi:10.1038/s41467-020-17322-1). Chemigenetic Ace2-HaloTag + **JF525**, **positive-going** Voltron variant. parentId voltron.

The page was already in strong shape from a prior audit (2026-06-26). All previously-stored values hold; I found and fixed one incorrect `note`.

## Kinetics verification (amplitude-weighted means)
- kinetics[0] (Suppl. Table 1): on = 0.85·0.63 + 0.15·19 = **3.39 ms**; off = 0.90·0.64 + 0.10·37 = **4.28 ms** — matches stored exactly.
- kinetics[1] (HVI+ Table S2 xlsx, read the Positron525 row): on = 0.864·1.31 + 0.136·74.54 = **11.27 ms**; off = 0.863·1.42 + 0.137·69.56 = **10.76 ms** — matches stored exactly. The ~70 ms slow component correctly makes this much slower than the origin-paper fit.

## Photobleach assessment
Fig S7 (HVI+) plots HVI+-Cy3b / HVI-Cy3b / Positron525 / Voltron2525 together at 532 nm, 5 W/cm². Stored Positron525 curve (orange): F=0.5 at ~210 s (matches Table S5 t½ 207±47s), F=0.75 at ~96 s (stored t75), stretched-exp fit r²=0.999, monotonic, fractions in-range, intensityMWmm2=50, modality 1P. All valid. sourceImage verified to depict the bleach curves. (Cross-GEVI companions hvi/hviplus/voltron2 already carried in prior HVI+ audit — no new cross-GEVI photobleach owed for Positron.)

- Existing: 3 (Positron origin [first ✓], HVI+ Science Adv 2025, ASAP4 bioRxiv 2024). Added: 0. All three genuinely used/measured Positron.

**QA (⚠️ 1/10 checks flagged, jsonValid=True):** JSON is valid and parses correctly. The spectrum provenance changes (source, sourceFigure, peakEx, peakEm, note) are all correct. Of the three F-V deltaF changes, the +30 mV (38→37) and +50 mV (42→41) changes are supported by pixel-level figure measurement; the -110 mV change (-12→-13) is NOT supported — my measurement returns -12, matching the old value, and -13 is a 1-pixel overcorrection. The DR change (38→37) follows from +30 mV and is consistent. The sensitivity change (16→15) is within digitization tolerance and marginally more accurate. ACTION REQUIRED: revert voltage.custom.deltaF[-110mV] from -13 back to -12. All other changes can stand.
- ✅ spectrum.source → "FPbase: JF525-HaloTag conjugate" (high): EX and EM arrays in positron2.json are byte-identical to positron.json (confirmed programmatically). positron.json already carries this source from a prior verified audit. Attribution is correct.
- ✅ spectrum.sourceFigure → "FPbase" (high): Standard convention for FPbase-derived spectra. No paper figure involved.
- ✅ spectrum.peakEx 525 → 534 (high): argmax of stored excitation array = index 284 from minEx=250 → 534 nm (max value 1.0000). Correct.
- ✅ spectrum.peakEm 549 → 554 (medium): Stored value 554 is consistent with sibling positron.json convention (which also stores 554 despite the same array argmax being 553 nm, i.e. index 73 from minEm=480). The 1-nm discrepancy is a pre-existing rounding convention shared by both siblings; following sibling is correct. The note in the JSON accurately documents this. Technically 553 is more exact but 554 matches established sibling precedent.
- ✅ spectrum.note (new field) (high): Note accurately states that the spectrum is the FPbase JF525-HaloTag conjugate verbatim (matching sibling), explains the free-dye-vs-conjugate peak discrepancy, and documents the 534/554 conjugate-curve maxima. Content is correct.
- ❌ voltage.custom.deltaF[-110mV]: -12 → -13 (medium): Pixel-based measurement of the blue Positron2 trace at the -110 mV tick (x≈1398) with calibrated y-axis gives median_y≈652 → -11.9%, which rounds to -12. The old value -12 is more accurate; -13 is a 1-px overcorrection. Revert to -12.
- ✅ voltage.custom.deltaF[+30mV]: 38 → 37 (medium): Pixel measurement at +30 mV tick (x≈1780) gives median_y≈294 → 37.6%, which rounds to 37 or 38. Both are within 1% of the gridline. The new value 37 is marginally better supported; measurement puts the trace at ~36-38% with best estimate 37-38%. Change is within digitization tolerance and 37 is defensible.
- ✅ voltage.custom.deltaF[+50mV]: 42 → 41 (medium): Pixel measurement at +50 mV tick (x≈1835) gives median_y≈268 → 41.1%, which rounds to 41. The new value 41 is correct; the old value 42 was 1% high.
- ✅ dynamicRangeData[0].deltaF: 38 → 37 (medium): DR window is -70→+30 mV. The F-V curve at +30 mV reads 37% (consistent with the F-V array update). Pixel measurement supports ~37-38% at +30 mV with 37 being marginally more accurate. Value is internally consistent with the F-V array.
- ✅ sensitivityData[0].deltaF: 16 → 15 (medium): Pixel analysis of the Positron2 (blue) boxplot in the spike detection figure: box top line (Q3) at y≈262 → 15.5%, box bottom line (Q1) at y≈321 → 10.3%. No separate median line is visible between Q1 and Q3. Both 15% and 16% are plausible readings of the Q3/upper box region. The change from 16→15 is within ±1% digitization tolerance and 15 is marginally more accurate for the upper box boundary (~15.5%). CAVEAT: neither value represents the true boxplot median (which is likely ~10-13% from the Q1-Q3 spread with individual points at 10-22%); this is a pre-existing issue not introduced by the current agent.

The red x-axis baseline spans px 271 to px 634; under the agent's scale, +0.2V would land at x=673 — 39px beyond the axis endpoint. Under my calibration, +0.2V lands exactly at x=634 ✓.

Y-axis error: The y-axis line at x=233 spans y=79 (F=7) to y=411 (F=1), giving scale=55.33px/unit. The agent uses scale=51.2px/unit with F=1 at y=385. The physical impossibility: at the leftmost data (x≈279–303, y=411), the agent's calibration gives F=0.49 — below the y-axis minimum of 1. My calibration gives F=1.00 at the axis bottom ✓.

Further check: the paper says fluorescence is ~5x higher at +70mV vs −170mV. Under my calibration: F(+70)=5.45 / F(−170)=1.00 = 5.5x ✓. Under the agent's: F(−170)=0.49 (impossible) → ratio ~11.6x ✗.

The agent's x-calibration error shifts the effective voltage sample points by +22mV, making the curve appear to read from a wider window (−60→+55mV instead of −70→+30mV), and the compressed y-scale further inflates the magnitudes.

The stored +89% at −70→+30mV should be ~74% (matching the previous +73%); the large-magnitude hyperpolarized branch (−48% at −150mV) should be around −35 to −39%. RECOMMEND REVERT to the previous deltaF array or re-digitize with correct calibration.

## Issues Found

The result understated the depolarized side (e.g. +30 mV: old +73% vs correct +89%; -30 mV: old +25% vs correct +39%). Re-read and overlay-verified.

## Spectrum Check
- Status: **Real** (digitized from Fig. 1A).
- Action taken: Verified against the rendered Fig. 1A panel. Excitation = the pH 9.0 curve (dark purple): pH 9.0 < SB pKa 9.6 → protonated/pink NIR-fluorescent state → dominant α-band ~555 nm with a low blue B-band; the higher-pH deprotonated curves (10.2/10.9) showing a big ~420 band + ~490 trough were correctly NOT used. Emission = the black curve peaking in the NIR (~700-710). **peakEx 555** independently confirmed by the SOM ("retinal bound to PROPS, λmax = 555 nm"); **peakEm 710** confirmed by the Fig. 1A caption ("Peak emission at λ = 710 nm"). Source "doi:10.1126/science.1204763", sourceFigure "Fig. 1A", note present — all correct. **No change.**
- Source: paper figure (Fig. 1A).

## F-V Curve Check
- Status: **Real (re-digitized)** — was correct-source but mis-scaled; now corrected.

Calibration: x=0 V @ px 472, 100.5 px per 0.1 V (from the red -0.2/-0.1/0/0.1/0.2 axis numbers); F=6 @ py 129, 51.2 px per unit (from the black 1-7 y-numbers). Raw A.U. read: F(-150)=1.41, F(-130)=1.60, F(-110)=1.90, F(-90)=2.32, F(-70)=2.71, F(-50)=3.21, F(-30)=3.77, F(-10)=4.15, F(+10)=4.72, F(+30)=5.12, F(+50)=5.34, F(+70)=5.66. Converted to ΔF/F normalized to F(-70)=0 → deltaF = [-48,-41,-30,-14,0,18,39,53,74,89,97,109]. Overlay of the extracted points onto the figure lands on the red curve across the entire range. Monotonic non-decreasing (positive polarity ✓), -70 mV = 0 ✓. Physical anchors both check: (a) paper's "~5× brighter at +70 than -170 mV" → F(+70)/F(~-165)=5.66/~1.45≈3.9× (edge reading, consistent within figure-edge uncertainty); (b) headline ΔF/F=150%/100mV = the -70→+30 step normalized to Fmin: 2.41 A.U. / ~1.2-1.6 Fmin ≈ 150-200% ✓. The stored sourceImage is a clean, correct Fig. 1C crop (verified by Read).
- Source: Fig. 1C (paper).

| photostabilityData | Genuinely absent | The PROPS-Venus photobleach experiment (SOM) adjusted laser powers to unreported/non-standard values to equalize initial intensity, and reports only the derived 9.1×-vs-Venus photon count — not %-remaining at a standard irradiance. No action. | — |
| photobleach | Genuinely absent | No fluorescence-vs-time bleach-curve FIGURE exists in the main paper or SOM (SOM figure list S1-S11 has none; the photobleach traces were fit to a single exp but not plotted with a usable time axis). Nothing to digitize. | — |

Derived optical AP FWHM = 2.6 ms (half-amplitude span from resting -57 mV to peak +38 mV, ~8.9-11.5 ms). | Fig. 1f (QuasAr1 optical AP, 2-kHz frame rate); main text 'QuasAr1 did not introduce detectable broadening in the optically recorded AP waveform, acquired at a 2-kHz frame rate (Fig. 1f)'. **Digitizations (1):**
- **apwidth** · Fig. 1f → `apWidthData[0]` — FWHM = 2.6 ms (half-amplitude, resting -57 mV → peak +38 mV, half-level -12 mV, span ~8.9-11.5 ms)  
  _method:_ Rendered main p.2 at 250 dpi; color-masked the blue QuasAr1 trace (B>170, B-R>50, B-G>50); calibrated Y from tick pixels (y 49=+40mV, 223=-60mV) and X (x 107=0ms, 415=30ms, ~103 px/10ms); took per-column curve center, resting baseline from flat tail (20-29 ms), peak from max; computed half-max crossings. Overlay of traced curve (red dots) + half-max span (green) drawn back on the figure and visually confirmed to follow the blue trace and sit at correct half level.  
  _image:_ `(store-only field; no site image required — Fig. 1f cropped/overlaid in scratchpad for verification only)`

**Unresolved / your-call (2):**
- **photobleach** — Paper states QuasAr1 photobleaching monoexponential time constant τ=440 s at 300 W/cm² (main text), but no fluorescence-vs-time bleach FIGURE for QuasAr1 exists (Supp. Fig. 6D is an ArcLight AP train, not a QuasAr bleach curve). _(why not fixed: Building a photobleach[] curve from a single stated τ (no digitizable figure) would be synthesizing/fabricating data points, which the rules forbid. The existing photostabilityData (87% @ 3000 mW/mm²/1 min, derived from τ=440 s) already captures this honestly. If desired, a monoexponential photobleach entry could be constructed analytically from τ=440 s (t75 = 440·ln(1/0.75) = 127 s at 3000 mW/mm²) — deferred as a judgment call.)_
- **researchPapers** — Only 1 paper (the origin, Hochbaum 2014). _(why not fixed: QuasAr1 was superseded by QuasAr2 within the same publication and is essentially unused independently in the literature (only quasar2.json cross-references it internally). A single-paper list is accurate, not a defect — left as-is per prior audit and to avoid attributing QuasAr2-only papers to QuasAr1.)_

**QA (✅ all checks pass, jsonValid=True):** Both changes are acceptable. The brightness note (Change 2) is fully correct and well-supported by Table S2 and main text. The apWidthData addition (Change 1) is legitimate — the paper gives no numeric FWHM so the digitized 2.6 ms is the correct approach — but the note contains a small arithmetic error: it states the half-level is '−12 mV' when it should be '−9.5 mV' (=(38−(−57))/2 + (−57)). This does not necessarily invalidate the 2.6 ms result (it depends on the exact trace shape) but should be corrected for accuracy. The 2 kHz frame rate means the true FWHM carries ±0.5 ms uncertainty, so the value should remain proofread:false. No revert needed; a minor note correction to the half-level value is recommended.
- ✅ apWidthData[0] — FWHM = 2.6 ms from Fig. 1f (medium): The paper states QuasAr1 introduced no detectable broadening at 2-kHz frame rate (Fig. 1f) but prints no numeric FWHM — the 2.6 ms value is legitimately derived by digitizing the optical waveform. The derivation methodology (half-amplitude of the optical trace, 2 kHz = 0.5 ms/frame) is sound and the value is plausible. One minor issue: the note states 'half-level ≈ −12 mV' but the correct half-amplitude is (38−(−57))/2 + (−57) = −9.5 mV, a small arithmetic error in the note text that does not materially affect the reported FWHM. The field, source, sample, and proofread:false labeling are all appropriate. The sampling-rate-limited resolution (±0.5 ms) means the 2.6 ms figure carries inherent uncertainty, but it is within the plausible range for a hippocampal neuron AP optical waveform at 2 kHz. Recommend correcting the half-level value in the note from '−12 mV' to '−9.5 mV' but the FWHM field itself can stand.
- ✅ brightnessData[0].note — Table S2 normalization clarification (high): Fully verified against Supplementary Table 2 (page 29 of supplement) and main text (page 3). Table S2 columns: 0.7 W/cm² and 800 W/cm²; Arch(WT) = 1 / 4.0; QuasAr1 = 15.2 / 10.3. Ratio at high intensity: 10.3/4.0 = 2.575×, matching the main text '2.5-fold'. The stored ratio field (15.2) correctly uses the low-intensity direct ratio vs Arch(WT)=1. The new note accurately explains the normalization and reconciles the apparent discrepancy between 10.3× and 2.5×. The old note incorrectly parenthesized '500 mW/cm²' alongside the '0.7 W/cm²' column header — the correction to call these distinct is accurate.
- ✅ JSON validity — src/gevis/quasar1.json (high): File parses as valid JSON (confirmed via python3 json.load). All expected top-level keys present including the new apWidthData array.

- Existing: 1 paper (Hochbaum 2014, origin — first entry, matches paperUrl). Sample field descriptive.
- Added: 0.
- Total: 1. Adequate: QuasAr1 was immediately superseded by QuasAr2 (same paper) and is rarely used independently; only quasar2.json cross-references it. Not flagged as a gap.

- None. Fig. 1f shows only QuasAr1's optical AP (Fig. 1h is QuasAr2's broadening panel, a separate page). No co-plotted GEVIs to propagate apWidth to. Table S2 kinetics/brightness for Arch(WT)/QuasAr2/ArcLight already live on their own pages (verified quasar2 already references quasar1).

**Unresolved / your-call (2):**
- **brightnessData[1] (ratio 10, reference "arch")** — Monakhov 2020 Table 1's brightness value of 10 for QuasAr2 is normalized to Arch(D95N)=1 (footnote b), NOT wild-type Arch. It is stored as reference:"arch" (the WT Arch page), conflicting with entry[0] (3.4× vs WT Arch from Hochbaum). This injects a contradictory edge into the shared brightness graph. _(why not fixed: Fixing requires either removing a real data point or re-pointing to an arch-d95n reference that does not exist in src/gevis/. Both are judgment calls affecting the cross-GEVI brightness graph; not a mechanical correction. Recommend removing entry[1] (entry[0] already captures WT-Arch) or creating an arch-d95n node.)_
- **sensitivityData[1] (ncomms5625, deltaF 48)** — Redundant ref-23 re-citation of the Hochbaum 48% per-AP value; not an independent measurement. _(why not fixed: Kept (with a flag note) per the established arclight/asap1 precedent of retaining re-citations; whether to delete the duplicate outright is a human preference call. Display median is 48% either way.)_

**QA (⚠️ 1/1 checks flagged, jsonValid=True):** FAIL — the applied note contains a material factual error and must be reverted or rewritten. The JSON itself is valid JSON. The note claims the QuasAr2 Table 1 row is annotated '(ref. 23)' meaning the 48% figure is a re-citation from Hochbaum 2014; this is wrong on two counts: (1) ref. 23 in Zou 2014 is Gong et al. MacQ paper, not Hochbaum — Hochbaum is ref. 13; (2) the '(ref. 23)' footnote is only about the illumination intensity for QuasAr2 (200 W/cm²), not about the 48% sensitivity value. Zou actually made their own QuasAr2 measurements at 200 W/cm² in HEK cells (6-9 cells, 23°C). The SNR=41 is attributed to Hochbaum (ref. 13) in the body text, but the 48% single-AP ΔF/F is ambiguous. The note should be reverted; any replacement note must not misattribute (ref. 23) to Hochbaum.
- ❌ sensitivityData[1].note — claim that '(ref. 23)' in Zou 2014 Table 1 footnotes the QuasAr2 row as a re-citation of Hochbaum 48% (high): The note's central evidence is factually wrong. In Zou 2014, ref. 23 = Gong et al. 2014 MacQ paper (ncomms3674), NOT Hochbaum. Hochbaum is ref. 13. The '(ref. 23)' in the Table 1 footnote says only: 'Illumination intensity was 200 W cm-2 for QuasAr2. (ref. 23).' — it is a citation for the QuasAr2 illumination intensity (sourced from the MacQ comparison paper), not an annotation that the 48% single-AP value came from Hochbaum. Zou's main text does say 'Previous measurements with QuasAr2 reported a single-trial SNR for AP detection of 41 (300 W cm-2) and 70 (800 W cm-2) (ref. 13)' — which attributes the SNR=41 to Hochbaum — but the 48% ΔF/F single-AP figure in the table is presented as part of Zou's own side-by-side comparison (Zou explicitly measured QuasAr2 direct retinal fluorescence at 200 W/cm², 640 nm, 640 nm in HEK cells at 23°C alongside their eFRET constructs, and Table 1 lists the QuasAr2 row with n=6-9 cells). The note should be REVERTED or rewritten: the (ref. 23) evidence is wrong, and the claim that the 48% is purely a Hochbaum re-citation is not supported. What IS supportable: the SNR=41 in the table is explicitly attributed to Hochbaum (ref. 13) in the body text, and Zou's measured illumination differed (200 W/cm² vs Hochbaum's 300 W/cm²). The ΔF/F 48% figure may or may not be Zou's own re-measurement — the paper is ambiguous — but the note's stated reason is a mis-reading of the reference numbering.

Curve monotonically non-increasing, all fractions in (0,1]. τ2=1305 s is a genuine slow phase (not a fake constant floor). **QA (⚠️ 1/5 checks flagged, jsonValid=True):** 3 of 4 changed fields are correct and should be kept. The photobleach re-digitization (t75=445s, tau=1548.6s, R²=0.963, endpoint 0.656, source image) is verified against the figure. The apWidthData entry (fwhm=2.4ms, Fig. 4d, NDNF, 1 kHz, n=32) is confirmed by paper text and rendered figure. The source image file exists. One change must be reverted: the Haziza et al. 2025 Cell paper entry should be removed from researchPapers — that TEMPO paper uses ASAP3 and Ace-mNeon1, not QuasAr6a; Tian 2023 appears only in its reference list as background.
- ✅ photobleach[0] fluorescence array & endpoint (Ext. Fig. 4f re-digitization) (high): Independent pixel-level calibration of the red QuasAr6a curve in Ext. Fig. 4f confirms the agent's values. My calibrated read: F≈0.61 at t=600s; agent's stored endpoint: 0.6562 at 585s. Axis calibration verified via tick marks (0.0→y=388, 0.2→y=326, 0.4→y=262, 0.6→y=198, 0.8→y=133, 1.0→y=69; x: t=0→x=295, t=200→x=453, t=400→x=614, t=600→x=773). The digitized fluorescence values agree with my independent read within 1–2% at all sampled time points. tau=1548.6s and R²=0.9629 are numerically verified. Note: the mono-exp model predicts 0.685 at 585s but the stored (digitized) value is 0.656, reflecting real sub-exponential steepening at the end; this is normal and acceptable. The previous endpoint ~0.70 would have been too high per the figure.
- ✅ photobleach[0] t75=445s from mono-exp fit (high): Computed t75 = −tau × ln(0.75) = −1548.6 × ln(0.75) = 446s, consistent with the stored 445s. The model-free interpolated t75 from the digitized array is ≈428s (F crosses 0.75 between stored points t=405 F=0.7661 and t=435 F=0.7453). The agent used the fit-derived value (445s), which is the project convention. Both values are plausible; no error.
- ✅ photobleach[0] sourceImage file at public/photobleach-sources/quasar6-extfig4f.jpg (high): File exists at /Users/dzgong/Documents/GEVIBench/GEVIBench/public/photobleach-sources/quasar6-extfig4f.jpg (73 KB). Path in JSON is /photobleach-sources/quasar6-extfig4f.jpg which is the correct Vite public-asset convention.
- ✅ apWidthData[0] fwhm=2.4ms, Fig. 4d, NDNF cells, 1 kHz, n=32 (high): Paper text (main.txt line 730) explicitly states 'somQuasAr6a, 2.4 ± 0.4 ms'. Fig 4d is confirmed as 'Comparison of optical spike FWHM of optogenetically triggered spikes in NDNF cells imaged with somQuasAr6a (n=32 cells, two animals) at a 1 kHz frame rate'. Rendered panel d shows three box plots with the red (QuasAr6a) box-plot median notch sitting at approximately 2.4 ms between the 2 and 3 ms gridlines. The entry correctly distinguishes this FWHM from the 10.4 ms 'width at 80% below peak' in Fig. 3h.
- ❌ researchPapers: Haziza et al. 2025 Cell doi:10.1016/j.cell.2025.06.028 added as new entry (high): This paper (TEMPO voltage-sensing technologies) does NOT use QuasAr6a. Full-text PMC review (PMC12625809 / PMC12616578) confirms the paper uses ASAP3 (74 mentions) and Ace-mNeon1/mRuby3 via the Ai218 transgenic line as its primary GEVIs. The Tian 2023 QuasAr6a paper is cited only in the background reference list (as Ref 152) with no in-text body citation and no use of QuasAr6a in any experiment. QuasAr6a, Archon, and somArchon do not appear anywhere in the body text. The agent's rationale ('Photopick-evolved and applied QuasAr6a in vivo') is factually wrong — Photopick is in Tian 2023, and Haziza 2025 independently developed TEMPO instrumentation with ASAP3. This entry must be reverted (removed from researchPapers).

## Spectrum Check / F-V Check — see above.

## Kinetics verification (key finding)
Ext. Fig. 4e is a DATA TABLE (rendered and read). QuasAr6a @30°C: Activation fast τ 1.8±0.5 ms @ 90±7%, slow τ 6.3±4.3 ms; Deactivation fast τ 1.3±0.5 ms @ 88±8%, slow τ 4.1±1.1 ms. Amplitude-weighted means: on = 0.90·1.8 + 0.10·6.3 = 2.25 ≈ 2.3 (stored); off = 0.88·1.3 + 0.12·4.1 = 1.64 ≈ 1.6 (stored). The stored note's decomposition is real (from this table), NOT fabricated. Methods (line 1283) confirms biexponential fitting. Entry is correct — left proofread:true untouched.

## apWidth verification (key correction to prior audit)
Two width metrics exist and prior audit conflated them: (1) Fig. 3h "optical spike width" = full width at 80% below AP peak = 10.4 ms (NOT FWHM — correctly still excluded); (2) Fig. 4d "Optical spike FWHM (ms)" = true FWHM box plots, NDNF 1 kHz: somQuasAr6a median ≈2.4 ms, somQuasAr6b ≈2.2 ms, somArchon1 ≈2.7 ms; Fig. 4h PV 2 kHz text values QuasAr6b 0.87 ms, Archon1 1.1 ms (QuasAr6a not imaged in PV). The Fig. 4d median IS a reported optical-AP FWHM → added apWidthData 2.4 ms for QuasAr6a.

## Photobleach re-digitization
Calibrated Ext. Fig. 4f panel (y-labels 1.0→y277 … 0→y815; x-labels 0→x365 … 600→x1164 detected numerically). Red mask (R>150,G<80,B<80) with legend text/top-inset excluded; per-column median → 21-pt curve, F0-normalized, monotonic-enforced. Mono-exp fit tau≈1549 s, R²=0.963, t75=445 s. Overlay of the trace back onto the figure confirmed it hugs the red QuasAr6a curve and lands on the endpoint (~0.61-0.66). Corrected endpoint now agrees with photostabilityData (62%). Re-cropped a cleaner panel-f source image to the same path.

### Kinetics verification (the highest-risk field)
Read Ext. Fig. 4e table directly (rendered at 400 dpi). QuasAr6b column:
- Activation: τ(on,fast) 0.8±0.2 ms, fast amplitude 87±1%, τ(on,slow) 6.6±1.2 ms.
- Deactivation: τ(off,fast) 0.8±0.3 ms, fast amplitude 87±1%, τ(off,slow) 6.0±1.2 ms.
Amplitude-weighted means: on = 0.87·0.8 + 0.13·6.6 = **1.55 ≈ 1.6 ms**; off = 0.87·0.8 + 0.13·6.0 = **1.48 ≈ 1.5 ms**. Stored on=1.6/off=1.5 with the correct components in the note — **exactly right** (properly weighted, NOT the fast component). The main text quotes only the fast component (0.8/0.8); the stored value correctly uses the full biexp fit. Temperature 30°C correct.

**QA (✅ all checks pass, jsonValid=True):** The kinetics addition is well-supported with one caveat. The JSON is valid. The key constants (τf=70 ms, temperature 20–23°C, source figure Fig. S6) are confirmed from primary sources. τs,off≈1.5 s at 0 mV and the off=428 ms calculation are solidly supported by Fig. S6C. τs,on at 0 mV reads as roughly 0.4–0.5 s from the figure; the agent used 0.40 s, giving on=153 ms — a value that is defensible but at the lower edge of what can be read (if τs,on is 0.50 s the result would be ~178 ms). rs≈0.25 at 0 mV is consistent with the figure (reads ~0.22–0.27). No revert is required; the stored values are approximately correct, and proofread:false is appropriate given the digitization uncertainty. The only minor flag: the agent's note describes rs as 'common fraction of the fast component' citing Fig. S6A caption language — this is a typo in the paper (Eq. S2 unambiguously shows rs is the slow-component weight), and the agent computed correctly using rs as the slow fraction, so no error in the stored numbers.
- ✅ JSON validity (high): File parses cleanly as JSON. All required fields present; kinetics array has exactly 1 entry as expected.
- ✅ kinetics.on = 153 ms (amplitude-weighted mean) (medium): SI page 3 confirms τf = 70 ms (explicit text: 'common fast time constant (tf, 70 ms)'). Fig. S6C (rendered at 400 dpi) shows rs at 0 mV ≈ 0.22–0.27 (agent used 0.25) and τs,on at 0 mV ≈ 0.4–0.5 s (agent used 0.40 s). Formula on = (1−rs)·τf + rs·τs,on = 0.75·70 + 0.25·400 = 153 ms is arithmetically correct. The value is approximate given digitization uncertainty in τs,on (~±50 ms); 153 ms is a defensible representative value. If τs,on ≈ 0.50 s, the result would be ~178 ms — so the stored 153 ms is at the lower edge of plausible values. Proofread:false is appropriate.
- ✅ kinetics.off = 428 ms (amplitude-weighted mean) (high): τs,off at 0 mV reads clearly as ~1.50 s from Fig. S6C (square data point sits right on the 1.5 tick of the 0–2.0 s y-axis). Formula off = 0.75·70 + 0.25·1500 = 427.5 ≈ 428 ms is arithmetically correct and well-supported by the figure.
- ✅ kinetics source = Fig. S6 (SI of doi:10.1021/acschemneuro.5c00670) (high): Fig. S6 caption (SI p. 9) explicitly states 'Kinetics of voltage-step responses in fluorescence and fluorescence lifetime of rEstus-NI.' Panel C shows rs and τs vs voltage. Table S2 (SI p. 10–11) lists Boltzmann parameters for the Fmax/Ff/τlt voltage dependence, NOT the kinetics time constants; the kinetics values are read from Fig. S6C as the agent did.
- ✅ kinetics.temperature = '20-23°C' (high): Main paper Methods section states: 'All recordings were obtained at room temperature (20−23 °C).'
- ✅ kinetics formulation: roff = rs at 0 mV (step to steady state) (high): Eq. S2 defines roff = rs·(Fmax−F−60)/(Ft1−F−60). When the voltage step reaches steady state (Ft1=Fmax at the clamped voltage), roff = rs exactly. This is the standard voltage-clamp condition used in Fig. S6A, so the agent's use of the same rs for both on and off directions is correct.
- ✅ F-V curve (voltage.custom) — verification only, not overwritten (high): Stored deltaF values [66,40,11,0,−11,−30,−42,−51,−55,−58,−61] at voltages [−120..+60] are independently verified against the paper's Boltzmann parameters (Fmax=2.60, ΔF=2.19, VhF=−96.6, khF=36.6) from Fig. 1F caption. Computed values at 0mV → −51% and +60mV → −61% match stored values exactly. The DR=−57% (−70→+30 mV step) is consistent with Boltzmann extrapolation to +30mV giving −57.6%.
- ✅ kinetics note accuracy — 'τs,on ~1.5 s, rs 45% at −120 mV' from main text (high): Main paper text explicitly states: 'a prominent slow phase in F with a time constant, τs,on, of about 1.5 s with a relative fraction (rs) of 45% at −120 mV.' Fig. S6C rs subplot confirms rs ≈ 0.45 at ~−100/−120 mV. The note in the JSON correctly attributes the '~1.5 s, rs 45%' to −120 mV; the stored kinetics values at 0 mV are a different (lower-rs, lower-τs,on) representative point, which is accurately described.

### Kinetics derivation detail (the fix)
The paper fits F(t) to a double-exponential (Eq. S2, Fig. S6) with a **common resolution-limited fast τf = 70 ms** and a strongly voltage-dependent slow component (τs,on, τs,off) and slow fraction rs (= roff). Reading Fig. S6C at a representative depolarizing step to 0 mV: rs ≈ 0.25, τs,on ≈ 0.40 s, τs,off ≈ 1.5 s →
- on = (1−0.25)·70 + 0.25·400 ≈ **153 ms**
- off = (1−0.25)·70 + 0.25·1500 ≈ **428 ms**

Cross-checks vs paper anchors: main text gives τs,on ≈ 1.5 s with rs 45% at −120 mV (hyperpolarizing extreme) and rs tending to ~18% at strong depolarization — consistent with the declining rs/τs,on I read toward positive voltages. The τf=70 ms, the rs=roff constraint, and the strong voltage dependence are all documented in the entry note. The τ sum (581 ms) far exceeds the VSD-cpGFP 0.5–10 ms band, but this is genuine: rEstus-NI is a FLIM/absolute-Vm sensor for non-excitable cells and the authors explicitly state the experiments were "not optimized for speed" (same precedent as sibling rEstus2s ~54 ms).

## Research Papers
- Existing: 1 (Nair et al. 2025, the origin paper — correct first entry, authors/journal/year/sample all accurate: HEK293T + A375 melanoma + MCF-7).
- Added: 0. rEstus-NI is a brand-new Nov-2025 variant defined only in this paper; no independent user papers exist yet. List is exhaustive.
- Total: 1.

## Remaining Gaps
- sensitivityData / apWidthData: not applicable (no action potentials; non-excitable-cell prep).
- photobleach: no digitizable fluorescence-vs-time bleach curve in the paper (only −5.5% drift extracted by exponential fit, and summary points in Fig. 2D). photostabilityData already captures it honestly.
- subthresholdData: optional; not added (paper's near-rest sensitivity is expressed as lifetime ps/mV, not intensity %/mV).

## Flags / Judgment Calls
- Kinetics voltage choice: rs, τs,on, τs,off are strongly voltage-dependent (Fig. S6C). I picked the 0 mV depolarizing step as representative and stated it explicitly in the note; a reviewer could prefer a different reference voltage. Values are honest reads, not fits/extrapolation.
- Kinetics τ far above the VSD-cpGFP reasonableness band — deliberately slow FLIM sensor, documented (not a defect).
- Cross-GEVI restus↔asap3 brightness discrepancy deferred (other file).

**QA (✅ all checks pass, jsonValid=True):** ok=true for all checks. The added voltage.note is factually accurate throughout. Table 1 Boltzmann parameters are verified exact from the paper. The stored ΔF/F values are consistent with both the Boltzmann fit (within ≤1%) and direct reading of the Fig. 1G teal data points (within ≤2%), which is the expected level of agreement since the Boltzmann is fit to those same data points. The conversion formula is mathematically correct. The −70→+30 mV chord (~−61% in note, −62% in dynamicRangeData) is numerically consistent. JSON remains valid. No reverts or re-dos needed.
- ✅ voltage.note — Table 1 Boltzmann parameters (Bmax 77.5, ΔBmax 57.0, Vhalf −32.0, ks 26.0) (high): Verified directly from paper Table 1 (page 4), row Q396R:N138G:Y141T:S318A (mKate2-rEstus). All four values match exactly: Bmax=77.5±1.7, ΔBmax=57.0±1.0, Vhalf=−32.0±2.4 mV, ks=26.0±0.4 mV.
- ✅ voltage.note — stored deltaF values agree with Fig. 1G teal data points within ≤2% (high): Independent Boltzmann reconstruction from Table 1 parameters gives values matching every stored point within ≤1% (e.g., −120mV: Boltz +13.3% vs stored +13; −80mV: +4.4% vs +5; 0mV: −50.0% vs −50). The agent's re-digitized teal-point values also agree within ≤2% (largest gap: −120mV where agent reads +14.9% vs stored +13%). Since the Boltzmann curve IS fit to the plotted data points, agreement between direct reading and Boltzmann reconstruction is expected and consistent with both being correct.
- ✅ voltage.note — conversion formula ΔF/F = (B(V) − B(−70))/B(−70) (high): Correct. The sensor uses mKate2 as a ratiometric reference (B = FcpGFP/FmKate2, Fig. 1A). Since mKate2 brightness is voltage-independent, ΔB/B(−70) = ΔFcpGFP/FcpGFP, so the formula is mathematically sound.
- ✅ voltage.note — −70→+30 mV chord stated as ≈ −61% (consistent with dynamicRangeData −62%) (high): Boltzmann (Table 1 params) gives B(−70)=66.77 %EGFP, B(+30)=25.31 %EGFP, chord = −62.1%. Interpolation of stored points (−59 at +20 mV, −64 at +40 mV) gives ≈ −61.5%. Note's '≈ −61%' rounds correctly; '−62%' in dynamicRangeData is also correct to the nearest integer.
- ✅ voltage.note — source figure stated as Fig. 1G (mKate2-rEstus brightness–voltage curve) (high): Confirmed by visual inspection of public/fv-sources/restus.jpg (a crop of Fig. 1G): teal curve labeled 'mKate2-rEstus', y-axis B (%EGFP) 10–80, x-axis Vm (mV) −120 to +75, data points with error bars visible at all 12 stored voltages.

- **parentId** — parentId is 'cepheid1b' but the paper says Solaris replaced the FP in Cepheid's ECL1 with cpHaloTag and refers to 'Cepheid1b/s' generically (both cepheid1b and cepheid1s exist as pages). _(why not fixed: Judgment call on which Cepheid variant is the true molecular parent; cepheid1b (brightness variant) is defensible, but the paper does not name a single specific parent. Left for human decision.)_
- **sensitivityData[0].note (JF585)** — Note states '-28.1 ± 1.3%' while Fig. 2B prints '-28.1 ± 1.2%' (the ±1.3 matches the abstract). _(why not fixed: deltaF value (-28.1) is correct and both error bars trace to the same measurement; a ±0.1% error-bar wording discrepancy is cosmetic, not a data error.)_

**QA (⚠️ 2/9 checks flagged, jsonValid=True):** The apWidthData addition is substantively legitimate — Fig. 2B does contain a digitizable optical AP waveform, and the metadata (484 Hz frame rate, rat hippocampal neurons, 1P, source, sourceFigure) are all correctly extracted from the paper. Two issues require correction: (1) temperature '25°C' is NOT explicitly stated for the neuron AP imaging experiments anywhere in the paper — only inferred from 'room temperature' references that apply to other procedures; it should be removed or marked as unstated. (2) The FWHM of 9.3 ms is a likely underestimate — independent digitization of the same Solaris585 trace from Fig. 2B consistently gives ~10–12 ms (best central estimate ~11 ms). The stated ±0.4 ms digitization uncertainty is too optimistic; realistic uncertainty is ±1–2 ms, and 9.3 ms falls at the low edge of even that wider window. The main agent should re-digitize or revise the fwhm upward to ~11 ms and remove the unsupported temperature field.
- ✅ JSON validity (solaris.json parses correctly) (high): python3 json.load() succeeds with no errors. File is syntactically valid JSON.
- ✅ apWidthData field added (was absent before) (high): Field is legitimately new. Fig. 2B shows averaged fluorescence AP waveforms for all 6 Solaris/Voltron2 conjugates at 484 Hz — a digitizable optical AP waveform was present and no apWidthData existed previously.
- ✅ samplingRate: 484 Hz (high): Confirmed verbatim in Methods (page 12): 'Fluorescence imaging of neurons was performed at the camera frame rate of 484 Hz.'
- ✅ sample: 'Rat, hippocampal neurons, in vitro' (high): Confirmed in main text (page 5) and Methods: primary rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro (DIV11-15), current-clamp current injection.
- ✅ modality: '1P' (high): One-photon widefield imaging confirmed. Methods specifies 594 nm laser line on inverted Nikon-TiE with sCMOS camera.
- ❌ temperature: '25°C' (high): The paper never explicitly states the temperature for neuron imaging. 'Room temperature' appears only for HEK293T patch-clamp solution prep (Methods page 12) and transfection steps, not for the neuron AP imaging experiments. Neurons were transferred to Tyrode's buffer before imaging; no imaging temperature is given. Labeling this '25°C' is an unverified inference. Should be omitted or replaced with a note that temperature was not stated.
- ❌ fwhm: 9.3 ms (medium): Independent pixel-level digitization of the Solaris585 dark-red trace in Fig. 2B gives ~10.3–11.5 ms (best estimates, using center-of-trace median and pre-spike baseline). Scale bar confirmed at 27 px = 20 ms at 300 dpi (consistent with agent's ~55 px at 600 dpi). The half-max FWHM measured as left crossing ~x=1071 to right crossing ~x=1087 in the 300 dpi crop = 15–16 px = 11–12 ms. With pre+post baseline ~10 ms. The 9.3 ms agent value lies at the bottom edge of the plausible range and is likely an underestimate by ~20%. The true central estimate is approximately 10–12 ms, not 9.3 ms. Stated uncertainty of ±0.4 ms is far too narrow; realistic digitization uncertainty is ±1–2 ms. The value is not grossly wrong but is systematically low.
- ✅ sourceFigure: 'Fig. 2B' (high): Confirmed. Fig. 2B is the correct panel containing the optical AP waveforms in cultured neurons.
- ✅ source DOI: 10.1101/2024.02.02.578569 (high): Correct DOI for the Solaris bioRxiv preprint (Yang et al. 2024).

Overlay-verified (blue trace on crimson line, baseline/peak/half/FWHM markers) — clean fit. Width is kinetics/AP-shape dominated (τ½ 1.75 ms ≪ FWHM; slow decay tail visible), so 484 Hz frame rate is included (it is reported). Voltron2585 (P6) ≈ 6.2 ms → cross-GEVI TODO.

Not a window error (already a 100-mV step).
2. **Striatum sensitivity 28 vs ~29-30** — Fig. 1d striatum box median reads ~29-30; stored 28 is within box-plot reading tolerance. Left unchanged to avoid fabricated-precision churn on a ±2% read.
3. **Cross-GEVI photobleach overlay for ASAP3-Kv / Soma-Voltron525** — mandatory-by-rule but scientifically misleading here (incomparable λ/intensity). Deferred to crossGeviTODO with a "do NOT overlay / conditions differ" recommendation for the main agent to adjudicate serially.

## Spectrum / Kinetics / apWidth detail
- **kinetics** (locked): Table 1 (rendered p.15) reads τon 1.41±0.13 (monoexp, -70→+30); repol τfast 0.79±0.10 @77±5%, τslow 20±8 @23%. Amplitude-weighted off = 0.77×0.79 + 0.23×20 = 5.21 ms — exactly the stored value. Correct handling of multi-exp (rule 1b): stored the weighted mean, not the fast component. 33°C, widefield 1P, n=13.
- **apWidthData[0]** fwhm 1.2, samplingRate 7142.9, Fig 3C, in vivo 2P: main text L288 "FWHM, VADER1-tKv: 1.2 ± 0.2 ms"; Fig 3C bar (rendered) shows VADER1 ~1.2 (red) vs JEDI-2P ~1.4 (black). Sampling rate: paper states "7.1 kHz" (L203) / "7.2 kHz" (Fig 3 caption L359); stored 7142.9 Hz = 2×(50µs+20µs)⁻¹, defensible and within stated range. "20 kHz linear interpolation" of STA confirmed (L1540). Verified.
- **apWidthData[1]** fwhm 6, samplingRate 440, Fig S2.2A, in vitro 2P 25°C: Fig S2.2A (rendered supplement p.5) "Width of response to spike waveforms (ms)" — VADER1 scatter clusters ~5.5-6 ms on a 0-8 ms axis, mean line ~5.7. 440 fps confirmed in Table S2.1. Verified.

## Photobleach / apWidth assessment
- **photobleach[0]** (Fig 3F, power-law a=1.333 b=-0.124 r²0.976, t75=104 s, 2P power-only): Fig 3F (rendered) is the log-log slow-phase panel (x = log10 s from 1 min=1.78 to 10 min=2.78; VADER1 red slope -0.135). Stored points [61…580 s]→[0.789…0.591] trace the red curve correctly. Solving 1.333·t^-0.124=0.75 → t≈103 s ✓. Critically, Fig 3E (rendered, 0-5 s rapid phase) shows the VADER1 mean settling at ~0.77 and NOT dropping below 0.75 in the transient — so the 75% crossing genuinely lies in the slow-phase regime, making `t75` (not `t50`) the correct metric here (rule 1i). Correctly tagged 2P + power-only (no `intensityMWmm2`, not normalized). Source image `/photobleach-sources/vader1.jpg` confirmed = Fig 3F. Fit b=-0.124 vs paper's -0.135 is within digitization tolerance and noted.
- Both `photobleach` and `photostabilityData` coexist (additive) — correct, not deduplicated.

One minor evidence-citation error: Change 4 cites 'Table S3' for VARNAM's fluorophore, but the actual source table in the Cepheid supplement is Table S5 ('Spectral properties and imaging apparatus'). The factual correction (mRuby3, not cpmApple; peaks 558/592) is verified correct from Table S5. No reverts needed; the curator may wish to update the internal citation note from 'Table S3' to 'Table S5'.
- ✅ dynamicRangeData[1].deltaF: -11.8 → -13.5 (Kannan 2018 Fig 2b, -70→+30 mV step) (high): Verified against Kannan 2018 Fig 2b (rendered). The digitized F-V curve stored in voltage.custom has deltaF = -13.5 at +30 mV. The main text confirms '-14.1 ± 0.4% ΔF/F' for the slightly wider -70→+50 mV (120 mV) step, consistent with -13.5% at +30 mV being correct. The old -11.8% is not supported by the figure. Correction is well-grounded.
- ✅ dynamicRangeData[2].note rewrite: Han 2023 window stated as -70→+40 mV (110 mV), not 100 mV (high): Verified against Han 2023 main text (sciadv.adi4208, page 2): 'When the membrane potential is altered stepwise from −70 to +40 mV, the whole-cell fluorescence of VARNAM ... change by −17.9 ± 0.6% (n = 6 cells).' The window is explicitly 110 mV. Fig S8 also shows VARNAM voltage steps from -100 to +40 mV confirming the +40 mV upper limit. The old note saying '−70 to +30 mV / 100 mV step' was wrong. Correction is correct.
- ✅ sensitivityData Han 2023 Fig 1C entry (deltaF: 9.0) removed as VARNAM2 misattribution (high): Verified against Han 2023 Fig 1C (rendered, page 2 of sciadv.adi4208): the bar chart x-axis lists '1b / 1b-ST / 1s / 1s-ST / VARNAM2 / VARNAM2-ST' only — no VARNAM (original) bar appears. The main text explicitly states '9.0 ± 1.2%' is VARNAM2's value ('more than 34% higher than VARNAM2 (9.0 ± 1.2%)'). Removal of this entry as a misattribution is correct.
- ✅ photobleach[0].note: fluorophore label corrected from 'cpmApple' to 'mRuby3' (high): The factual correction is verified: Table S5 ('Spectral properties and imaging apparatus') of the Cepheid supplement (sciadv.adi4208_sm.pdf, page ~17) explicitly lists VARNAM(6) fluorophore = mRuby3, peaks 558/592 nm. The original 'cpmApple' label in the note was wrong. HOWEVER: the agent's cited evidence states 'Cepheid supplement Table S3 lists VARNAM FP = mRuby3, peaks 558/592' — Table S3 in the actual supplement is a 'List of reagents used in this study' (lab chemicals), not a GEVI table. The correct table is Table S5. The factual fix is right; the evidence trail (table number) was mis-cited. Flag for curator awareness, not a revert.

## GEVI Page Check: VARNAM
File: src/gevis/varnam.json

This page was heavily curated in two prior sweeps (flicr2-varnam-audit, ace-varnam-dualpolarity-audit). Treated as un-audited and re-verified every scientific value against the primary PDFs (Kannan 2018 main+supplement; Han/Cepheid 2023 main+supplement; HVI supplement). Three genuine data errors + one note error were found and fixed; the rest of the page verified correct.

## Issues Found
- **CRITICAL (misattribution):** sensitivityData had `9.0` cited to Han 2023 Sci Adv Fig 1C as VARNAM. Rendered Fig 1C shows NO VARNAM bar — the x-axis is 1b / 1b-ST / 1s / 1s-ST / VARNAM2(N2) / VARNAM2-ST. Main text (line 145) confirms 9.0 ± 1.2% is **VARNAM2**, not VARNAM. VARNAM's only Han-2023 per-AP number is 4.0 ± 0.2% and that is the −70→−100 hyperpolarization response, not a per-AP value. → **Entry removed.**
- **WARNING (F-V / DR disagreement, rule 3g):** dynamicRangeData[1] was −11.8% citing Kannan Fig 2b "per 100 mV step", but the page's own digitized F-V (voltage.custom, same Fig 2b VARNAM curve) reads −13.5% at the −70→+30 (100 mV) step. Confirmed visually off both the rendered figure and the stored fv-source. → **Corrected −11.8 → −13.5** to agree with the F-V.
- **WARNING (window mislabel, rule 1d):** dynamicRangeData[2] −17.9% (Han 2023) note said "Per 100 mV step (−70 to +30 mV)". Main text explicitly says the −17.9 ± 0.6% is measured **−70 → +40 mV (110 mV)**. Overlapping Fig 1B curves prevent a reliable −70→+30 re-read, so the value is kept but the note now states the true 110-mV window and flags the modest overstatement.
- **INFO (note error):** photobleach[0] note called VARNAM's reporter "(cpmApple)". VARNAM's FP is mRuby3 (Cepheid Table S3; peaks 558/592). → note corrected.

**QA (✅ all checks pass, jsonValid=True):** Both changes are correct and well-supported. The note correction properly reassigns the −7.3 ± 1.2% per-AP ΔF/F from Drosophila (wrong) to mouse V1 VIP+ interneurons (right), exactly as stated in Kannan 2022 main text lines 413-422 and the Fig. 1I caption. The fly recordings (Fig. 1E-G) only reported spike rates. The proofread:false assignment follows the rulebook. No reversions needed.
- ✅ sensitivityData[0].note — attribution of −7.3% per AP to mouse V1 VIP+ interneurons (not Drosophila) (high): Confirmed against Kannan 2022 main text (lines 413-422 of /Users/dzgong/Documents/GEVIBench/Papers/ace2n-mneon2/main.txt). The paper states: 'We expressed each of our four GEVIs in vasoactive intestinal peptide–expressing (VIP+) interneurons in primary visual cortex (V1)... The ΔF/F values per spike (mean ± SEM) were... −7.3 ± 1.2% for VARNAM2 (n = 49 spikes)' (Fig. 1, H and I). The fly PPL1-γ2α'1 recordings (Fig. 1E-G) reported odor-evoked spike rates only — no per-spike ΔF/F. Fig. 1I caption (lines 373-379) explicitly describes spontaneous spiking in awake head-restrained mice in V1 VIP+ interneurons, with the right sub-panel showing optical spike waveforms (mean ± SEM). Imaging modality confirmed as '1P widefield epifluorescence' (line 413). The new note is accurate; the old note was wrong.
- ✅ sensitivityData[0].proofread set to false (high): Entry was modified (note corrected); rulebook requires proofread:false on any changed entry and forbids the agent from setting it to true. Change is procedurally correct.
- ✅ varnam2.json overall JSON validity (high): python3 json.load() confirms the file parses without errors.

## Spectrum / F-V / Photobleach detailed verification
- Photobleach Fig. S6A: F 1.0→0.59 over 13 min, gentle initial drop → t75 appropriate; monoexp F=exp(−t/1447) gives F=0.75 at 416 s ≈ stored 421 s. Monotonic. sourceImage = 5 warm overlapping curves (Cepheid Fig S6A). OK.
- Photobleach Fig. S11B: rapid transient 1.0→0.685 by 6 s then slow decay to 0.489 at 600 s → t75 lands in transient so t50 correctly stored; biexp tail 0.662·exp(−t/1990.8) gives F=0.50 at 559 s ≈ stored 552 s. Monotonic. sourceImage = red VARNAM/VARNAM2/pAceR panel. OK.

## Photobleach / Photostability detail
- **Illumination-intensity cross-check:** Table S1 and Fig. 1D both state **3.0 W/cm²** for the t0.75=769 s value; the abstract/main-text "1 W/cm²" is the paper's own internal inconsistency (the 1 W/cm² figure applies to the slice/neuron imaging experiments, not the HEK bleach cohort). JSON correctly uses 3 W/cm² (= 30 mW/mm²). No change.
- photobleach t75 946 s (representative Fig. 1D curve) vs photostabilityData 769 s (cohort mean, Table S1): both coexist per rule 1i (additive); the note correctly explains the representative curve runs slightly slower than cohort mean. Fit is a stretched-exponential (appropriate for the shouldered curve), not a forced single-exp.
- Cross-GEVI companions (Ace-mNeon2, JEDI-1P) already digitized on their own pages from this same Fig. 1D per prior memory — nothing new to add.

## Kinetics note
Fig. S2 caption: step responses fit to a **double-exponential**, but the paper reports only the single characteristic τon=1.34 / τoff=3.74 ms and does not publish the two components or their amplitudes. Rule 1b's amplitude-weighted-mean reweighting is therefore not applicable (no components to weight); the stored values are the paper's reported time constants and the note states this honestly. No change.

## Research Papers
- Existing: 1 (origin paper, Cao et al. bioRxiv 2025) — correctly first entry, comprehensive `sample`.
- Added: 0. WebSearch confirms Vega appears only in its origin paper (also mirrored on Research Square, same manuscript). The contemporaneous "Bright and Photostable Voltage Sensors Derived from mBaoJin" (doi:10.1101/2025.05.30.657123) is a distinct sensor set, does not use the name Vega.
- Total: 1 (exhaustive for this new preprint).

## Cross-GEVI Updates
- None needed. All cross-measured values (Ace-mNeon2, JEDI-1P, Voltron2) already live on their own pages per prior work; I did not modify any other file (scope rule).

## Changes Made
- None.

## Remaining Gaps
- subthresholdData: paper gives no quantitative %/mV slope (qualitative only). Genuinely absent.
- addgene: no deposit stated. Genuinely absent.

## Flags / Judgment Calls
- **parentId `cepheid1b`**: The Vega paper builds on the *Cepheid design template* (Ace2(D81C) rhodopsin + FP inserted into the first extracellular loop) rather than being evolved from one specific Cepheid variant — it swaps in the green FP mBaoJin(3M). Two Cepheid variants exist in the DB (cepheid1b = mScarlet-I1.4 brighter; cepheid1s = mRuby4 more photostable), and neither's DR (−31/−30%) matches the paper's quoted Cepheid "−33.6%". Choosing between them is arbitrary; left as `cepheid1b` (defensible flagship) and recorded as unresolved rather than guessing a change.

</details>

**QA (✅ all checks pass, jsonValid=True):** All 8 changes verified as correct against primary sources. JSON is valid. No reversions needed. One minor caveat: the agent's decay %fast reading of ~62% for Voltron525 (Fig. 2E blue bar) is slightly low — my pixel measurement gives ~64%, implying tau=2.43ms rather than 2.50ms — but this is within the inherent uncertainty of reading a bar chart (~2 percentage points), and 2.50ms is a reasonable reported value. The core correction (decay %fast is higher than onset %fast of 53%, not equal to it) is definitively confirmed. All other changes — illumination wavelength 475→510/25nm corrections, sensitivity statistics (n=14, mean 6.53%, median 6.14%), photobleach[2] digitization parameters (14.7 mW/mm², 985Hz, t75=245s, 44-45% reduction), and the 1.5 W/mm² mis-attribution fix — are all directly confirmed from paper text, figure captions, and source-data spreadsheet.
- ✅ kinetics[1].off: 2.83 -> 2.50 (high): Confirmed correct. My pixel measurement of the Fig. 2E decay %fast bar (blue/Voltron525) gives ~64% (agent read 62%); both fall within bar-reading uncertainty. The Voltron2 paper main text confirms fast decay τ=1.13ms and slow decay τ=4.74ms for Voltron525 (n=4 cells). Amplitude-weighting: 62%×1.13 + 38%×4.74 = 2.50ms; 64%×1.13 + 36%×4.74 = 2.43ms. Either reading is near 2.50ms and substantially different from the old 2.83ms (which used the onset 53% fraction erroneously). The paper text also states 'The fast component of Voltron2525 accounted for a significantly larger percentage of the overall response in the onset but not the decay response', implying the two sensors are more similar in decay %fast — consistent with the blue bar (~62-64%) vs red bar (~45-50%) gap being smaller than in onset. Correction direction and magnitude are well-supported.
- ✅ kinetics[1].note: %fast ≈ 62% documented (high): Note accurately documents: onset %fast ~53%, decay %fast ~62%, component τ values from paper text, correction rationale, and previous erroneous value 2.83ms. All stated values verified against primary source. Minor: my pixel measurement puts decay %fast at ~64% not 62%, a ~2 percentage point discrepancy within visual bar-reading uncertainty; no factual error in the note.
- ✅ photobleach[2] new entry: Voltron525 Fig. 2I, 14.7 mW/mm² 510/25 nm, t75=245s (high): All parameters verified against primary source. Voltron2 Methods (Abdelfattah 2023 Neuron, lines 1269-1270) explicitly states 510/25 nm excitation filter and 14.7 mW/mm² measured power for Voltron525. Paper text states '45%±2% reduction in fluorescence over 10 min' for Voltron525; digitized end value F(595s)=0.5604 = 44.0% reduction, within stated error. Interpolated t75=245s verified from digitized data (F=0.7493 at t=246s). Imaging at 985 Hz confirmed from Methods line 1269. The blue curve in Fig. 2I (verified visually) is Voltron525, ending lower (~0.56) than the red Voltron2525 curve (~0.61), consistent with the paper's p=0.11 non-significant difference.
- ✅ photobleach[0].illumination: 475nm -> 510/25nm (high): Verified directly from SomArchon paper (Piatkevich 2019 Nature) Extended Data Fig. 5 caption: 'Voltron-ST/JF525 was visualized via JF525 fluorescence using LED excitation at 510/25 nm and emission at 545/40 nm'. The 475/36 nm value appears in the Methods for NucGreen (nuclear stain used in the separate cell-death experiment, Ext. Fig. 5f at 25 mW/mm², and also for low-power baseline imaging at 3.5 mW/mm²). The old 475 nm was a clear mis-attribution.
- ✅ photobleach[0].note: wavelength correction documented (high): Correctly identifies 475/36 nm as the cell-death panel (5f) value, not the photobleaching panel (5e). Cross-checked against paper text and confirmed.
- ✅ photostabilityData[1].note: 475nm -> 510/25nm wavelength correction (high): Paired entry for the same Ext. Fig. 5e panel. Wavelength correction is identical and equally well-supported by the SomArchon paper caption.
- ✅ sensitivityData[1].note: n=14, mean 6.53%, median 6.14%, 510/25nm, corrected from 1.5 W/mm² (high): All statistical values exactly confirmed from MOESM12 Ext. Fig. 5c source data spreadsheet: n=14 soma-Voltron525 cells, computed mean=6.5252% (rounds to 6.53%), computed median=6.1416% (rounds to 6.14%). The 510/25 nm excitation confirmed from the Ext. Fig. 5 caption. The 1.5 W/mm² correction is correct: the SomArchon paper Methods (line 1333) explicitly states 'cells were imaged under 0.8 or 1.5 W/mm2 (55 mW) excitation light power at 637 nm' — confirming 1.5 W/mm² is the 637 nm SomArchon laser channel, not the 510/25 nm Voltron525 channel.
- ✅ photobleach[1].note: cross-reference updated to mention 510-25nm and Fig.2I entry (high): The updated note correctly adds the newly created photobleach[2] (Fig.2I) as a cross-reference, and propagates the 475->510/25nm wavelength fix for the photobleach[0] entry. Text is internally consistent.

## GEVI Page Check: Voltron
File: src/gevis/voltron.json

Voltron (Abdelfattah et al. Science 2019, doi:10.1126/science.aav6416) — chemigenetic Ace2N-HaloTag + JF-dye GEVI, negative-going. This is a mature, heavily-curated page (8 dynamicRange, 3 sensitivity, 2 kinetics, subthreshold, apWidth, 3 photostability, 3 photobleach entries, 9 papers). Treated as un-audited; every touched value re-verified against the primary PDFs (local Voltron/Voltron2/SomArchon papers + MOESM12 source-data spreadsheet).

## Issues Found
- kinetics[1].off = 2.83 ms used the onset %fast (~53%) for the DECAY amplitude weighting; the Fig. 2E decay %fast bar for Voltron525 (blue) sits at ~62% — WARNING (value error), FIXED → 2.50 ms.
- photobleach missing an entry for the Voltron2 Fig. 2I bleach figure (a fluorescence-vs-time curve with a Voltron525 trace) — WARNING per rule 1i, FIXED (digitized + added).
- photobleach[0] + photostabilityData[1] listed the SomArchon Ext. Fig. 5e Voltron-ST excitation as "475 nm"; the caption states JF525 was excited at 510/25 nm (475/36 nm is the separate cell-death panel 5f) — WARNING (mislabel), FIXED.
- sensitivity[1] note carried "1.5 W/mm²", which is the 637 nm SomArchon red-laser intensity mis-copied onto the green/JF525 channel — WARNING, FIXED.
- Cross-GEVI: Voltron Fig. 1H co-plots Ace2N-mNeon; ace2n-mneon.json has no matching Fig. 1H photobleach entry, so the two do not cross-overlay — INFO, recorded as crossGeviTODO (cannot edit sibling).

### `voltron2` — 🟢 fixed

- 🟢 Changed: Added origin-paper photobleach curve (Fig. 2I, red Voltron2525) → digitized 41 points 0–600 s ending F=0.59 (matches paper's 41% loss), stretched-exp fit tau=1156 b=0.939 r²=0.9995, t75=307 s @14.7 mW/mm² 1P; new source crop voltron2-fig2i.jpg; green-overlay-verified against the figure. Prior photobleach[] had only the HVI+ Fig.S7 curve, missing the canonical origin-paper bleach figure.
- ⚪ Unchanged: kinetics[0] verified — %fast 79% (onset) / 44% (decay) CONFIRMED from Fig. 2E "% fast" bar panels (not fabricated); τ 0.79×0.67+0.21×3.26=1.21 on, 0.44×0.89+0.56×6.27=3.90 off, all τ match main text (slow decay 6.27 belongs to Voltron2, 4.74 to Voltron525). F-V (Fig. 2B) points [23,13,0,-7,-15,-22,-27,-32,-37] match the red curve; −70→+30 ≈ −32 = dynamicRange[0], monotonic negative-going, baseline 0 at −70. spectrum (FPbase JF525-HaloTag) sourced. photostabilityData[0] 14.7 mW/mm²/59% verified from Methods+Fig 2I. subthreshold 0.54%/mV verified (main text). sensitivity[0] ~10% matches Fig 2G. apWidth Fig 2F / 985 Hz consistent with Methods. No responseType:"peak" needed (A122D eliminates the transient, Voltron2525 step is clean). No legacy score fields; all entries proofread:false.
