Every verifiable claim — sensor identity (red curve = Voltron2525), endpoint F(600)=0.59, illumination 14.7 mW/mm², t75=307 s, fit parameters (tau=1156.3, b=0.939, R²=0.9995), x-axis calibration, and the 41 digitized points — was independently confirmed against Fig. 2I of Abdelfattah 2023 Neuron and the paper's Methods/Results text. The only minor note is that the agent's description of the y-axis range ("F=1.0..0.5") is slightly imprecise (actual labeled gridlines are 0.6–1.0), but the calibration itself is validated by the correct endpoint. No reversions needed.
- ✅ JSON validity (high): voltron2.json parses as valid JSON with no syntax errors.
- ✅ photobleach[0] source: Fig. 2I, Abdelfattah 2023 Neuron (high): Confirmed: Fig. 2I (page 5 of local PDF, doi:10.1016/j.neuron.2023.03.009) shows a photobleaching comparison of Voltron525 (blue) and Voltron2525 (red) over 0–600 s. Legend caption at line 298 of PDF text confirms 'Photobleaching comparison of Voltron525 and Voltron2525 over 10 min'.
- ✅ Red curve = Voltron2525 (correct sensor identity) (high): In Fig. 2I, red = Voltron2525 and blue = Voltron525, consistent with the figure color scheme used throughout the paper (Voltron2 always red, Voltron always blue). Curve labeled Voltron2_525 in JSON.
- ✅ Endpoint F(600 s) = 0.59 matching paper's stated 41% ± 1% loss (high): Paper text (line 368): 'Voltron2525: 41% ± 1% reduction in fluorescence over 10 min'. 41% loss → 59% remaining = F=0.59. Stored digitized fluorescence[40]=0.59 at time[40]=600 s. The endpoint is exact.
- ✅ Illumination 14.7 mW/mm² for photobleach[0] (high): Confirmed directly from paper Methods (line 1269): 'filter cube containing 510/25 nm excitation filter, 545/40 emission filter, 525 nm dichroic (Chroma), with a measured power of 14.7 mW/mm2'. Matches stored illumination field and is consistent with photostabilityData[0].illumination.
- ✅ t75 = 307 s (time to F=0.75) (high): Independently computed from stored digitized points: F crosses 0.75 between t=300 s (F=0.754) and t=315 s (F=0.744); linear interpolation gives 306.0 s. From fit parameters: tau=1156.3, b=0.939 → t75 = 1156.3 × (−ln 0.75)^(1/0.939) = 306.8 s. Both are consistent with the reported 307 s (rounded).
- ✅ Stretched-exponential fit: tau=1156.3, b=0.939, R²=0.9995 (high): Computed R² of F=exp(−(t/1156.3)^0.939) against the 41 stored digitized points = 0.9995, exactly matching the reported value. F(600) from fit = 0.583, close to but slightly below the stored 0.59 endpoint (difference ≤ 0.007, within digitization scatter). Fit is self-consistent.
- ✅ X-axis calibration: 65/425/785/1145 px = 0/200/400/600 s (high): Arithmetic check: 65 + (200/600)×1080 = 425, 65 + (400/600)×1080 = 785, 65 + 1080 = 1145. All three intermediate gridlines reconstruct correctly, confirming consistent linear calibration.
- ✅ Y-axis calibration description: 'y gridlines F=1.0..0.5 @0.1' (high): Minor inaccuracy in the description only: the figure y-axis labels show gridlines at 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1.0 (not down to 0.5); the plot area extends slightly below 0.6 to accommodate the 0.59 endpoint. However, the calibration itself is validated by the endpoint F(600)=0.59 matching the paper text exactly, so the actual numerical calibration used was correct regardless of the description.
- ✅ sourceImage path: /photobleach-sources/voltron2-fig2i.jpg (high): File exists at public/photobleach-sources/voltron2-fig2i.jpg. The image shows the correct panel (two photobleach curves with time axis 0–600 s and F axis). The crop is tight and missing the y-axis labels, but all gridlines are present for calibration.
- ✅ Digitized data: 41 points at 15 s intervals, monotonically decreasing (high): 41 time points [0, 15, …, 600], 41 fluorescence values [1.0, 0.988, …, 0.59]. Monotonically decreasing (verified computationally). Consistent with a 10-minute experiment sampled every 15 s.
- ✅ No spurious changes to pre-existing fields (kinetics, dynamicRange, spectrum, etc.) (high): CHANGES list was empty. Inspection of voltron2.json confirms all pre-existing fields (kinetics, dynamicRangeData, sensitivityData, voltage, spectrum, photostabilityData, etc.) are intact and unmodified. ## Kinetics Verification (1b)
The stored kinetics[0] (on 1.21, off 3.90, 23°C) is CORRECT and NOT fabricated:
- Rendered Fig. 2E and confirmed it plots **"% fast"** bars for both onset and decay. Voltron2525 (red): onset %fast ≈ 78–80%, decay %fast ≈ 44–45%. This directly supports the note's amplitude weights (79% / 44%).
- τ from main text: fast onset 0.67, slow onset 3.26; fast decay 0.89, slow decay 6.27 (the "vs 4.76 / vs 4.74" values belong to Voltron525 — on/off assignment in the note is correct).
- Weighted: onset 0.79·0.67 + 0.21·3.26 = 1.21 ms ✓; decay 0.44·0.89 + 0.56·6.27 = 3.90 ms ✓. Reasonableness: sum 5.1 ms, within chemigenetic 0.1–20 ms.

The voltage array trim (removing −200 and −150 mV) is justified — the leftmost scatter data in Fig. 3B is at approximately ΔVm −125 to −130 mV, clearly not reaching −150 or −200 mV. The provenance note and DR note update both accurately reflect the figure. Nothing to revert or redo.

No data points appear at −150 or −200 mV. The trim is correctly justified. Remaining points (−100 to +150 mV) all fall within the measured data span. Linear fit through stored points gives slope ~1.72%/100 mV, consistent with paper's 1.8 ± 0.1%/100 mV. −70 = 0 normalization correct. −70→+30 chord ≈ 1.72% consistent with dynamicRange 1.8%.
- ✅ voltage.note — provenance note added (high): Note content is accurate: Fig. 3B x-axis is ΔVm (confirmed from figure label), r=0.99 and 1.8%/100 mV are stated in paper text and caption, −70=0 re-referencing is correct, and removal of −200/−150 points is justified by the data span. The note is factually accurate and adds genuine transparency.
- ✅ dynamicRangeData[0].note — updated measured range from −150→+250 to ΔVm ≈ −125 to +210 mV (high): Old note used axis tick extents (−150/+250 mV) rather than the actual data marker span. Fig. 3B data markers do not reach −150 mV (leftmost marker ~−125 to −130 mV) and extend to approximately +200–210 mV on the right. Updating to ΔVm ≈ −125 to +210 mV is more accurate. The addition of 'ΔVm' clarifying the x-axis label is also correct and important (ΔVm = change in membrane potential, not absolute Vm).

## GEVI Page Check: VSFP1
File: src/gevis/vsfp1.json
Paper: Sakai et al. 2001, Eur J Neurosci 13:2314-2318 (doi:10.1046/j.0953-816x.2001.01617.x) — the FIRST genetically-encoded voltage indicator (Kv2.1 VSD + CFP/YFP FRET). Read in full from local PDF; Fig. 3 rendered and inspected.

## Issues Found
- [x] **F-V curve contained two extrapolated hyperpolarized points (−200, −150 mV)** beyond the figure's leftmost measured data (ΔVm ≈ −125 mV) — WARNING (no-extrapolation rule). FIXED (removed).
- [x] dynamicRange note stated the linear range as "−150→+250 mV" (the axis-tick extent, not the data span) and omitted that Fig. 3B's x-axis is ΔVm — INFO. FIXED (refined to measured ΔVm −125→+210 mV).
- [ ] researchPapers has only 1 entry — INFO, judged exhaustive (see Research Papers).
- No other defects: no legacy score fields; every checkable entry already carried `proofread: false`; sourceFigure formats correct; spectrum sourced; kinetics/DR/subthreshold values match the paper.

## Proofread Skip Summary
- Skipped because `proofread: true`: 0 entries (none were marked true)
- Added missing `proofread: false`: 0 (all present already)
- Force mode: n/a (treated page as un-audited per instructions)

## Spectrum Check
- Status: **Real (FPbase composite), correct — no change.**
- source "FPbase: ECFP / EYFP", sourceFigure "FPbase". Excitation array = FPbase ECFP (donor, cyan), peak at index 134 → 300+134 = **434 nm** (paper: CFP excitation 432 nm ✓; stored peakEx 435 ✓). Emission array = FPbase EYFP (acceptor, yellow), peak at index 34 → 495+34 = **529 nm** (paper: YFP emission ~530 nm ✓; stored peakEm 529 ✓). Both arrays internally consistent, asymmetric emission tail (not Gaussian), not corrupted (unlike ElectricPk). VSFP1 is read as the YFP/CFP FRET ratio → donor-ex + acceptor-em composite is the correct convention (matches sibling VSFP pages). Verified against Fig. 3F (dominant YFP peak ~527-530 nm + CFP shoulder ~480 nm). Note is accurate. No published 1P excitation spectrum exists for VSFP1 itself, so the FPbase composite is the right choice.

## F-V Curve Check
- Status: **Real (linear), had extrapolated endpoints → FIXED.**
- Fig. 3B plots ΔF/F (%) vs **ΔVm** (change in membrane potential, NOT absolute Vm), linear r=0.99, slope 1.8 ± 0.1%/100 mV (n=11). ~15 scatter markers span ΔVm ≈ −125 to +210 mV; the line crosses ΔF/F=0 at ΔVm≈+25 mV. VSFP1 has NO F-V saturation (Kv2.1 VSD activates very depolarized), so a linear curve is physically correct.
- Action: removed the two hyperpolarized points at −200 and −150 mV (deltaF −2.2, −1.4) that lay beyond the figure's leftmost data (~−125 mV) — these were slope extrapolations, disallowed. Kept the within-range linear points `[-100,-70,-50,0,50,100,150]` → `[-0.5,0,0.3,1.2,2.1,3.0,3.8]` (slope ≈1.72%/100 mV, within the paper's 1.8 ± 0.1). Monotonic non-decreasing ✓, −70 = 0 ✓, positive-going ✓ (matches voltage.polarity and dynamicRangeData.sign). −70→+30 chord ≈ +1.74% ✓ agrees with dynamicRange 1.8%.
- sourceImage /fv-sources/vsfp1.jpg opened and confirmed to be the Fig. 3B linear ΔF/F-vs-voltage curve (not a bar chart/Boltzmann). sourceFigure "Fig. 3B" ✓. Added a voltage.note documenting the ΔVm axis, the linear re-referencing (−70=0), and the extrapolation trim.

## Missing Performance Data
| Field | Status | Action | Source |
|-------|--------|--------|--------|
| kinetics | Present, correct | none — 0.74/0.74 ms @25-28°C is the paper's single instrument-limited τ (0.74±0.12 ms, n=19, identical for ±100 mV hyper/depol steps; Fig. 3E exp fit 0.7 ms). Not multi-exponential, so no amplitude-weighting applies (rule 1b OK). Within reasonable range. | doi:10.1046/…01617.x, Fig. 3E |
| dynamicRangeData | Present, correct | none — 1.8%/100 mV, sign positive, modality 1P (widefield HEK293), window is the −70→+30 100-mV step (linear F-V ⇒ per-100mV = slope). | Fig. 3B |
| sensitivityData | Genuinely absent | none — no per-AP data (HEK293 voltage-clamp only; no action potentials recorded). | — |
| brightnessData | Genuinely absent | none — paper reports no EC×QY or EGFP comparison for VSFP1. | — |
| photostabilityData | Genuinely absent | none — no photostability/bleach measurement. | — |
| subthresholdData | Present, correct | none — 0.018%/mV = 1.8%/100mV (linear F-V). | Fig. 3B |
| apWidthData | Genuinely absent | none — no optical single-AP waveform and no stated FWHM (no APs in this prep). | — |
| photobleach | Genuinely absent | none — Sakai 2001 has NO fluorescence-vs-time bleach figure. (Fig. 2A is FRET acceptor-photobleaching verification, not a photostability decay curve.) | — |

## Photobleach / apWidth assessment
- **photobleach:** genuinely absent — no bleach-vs-time figure in the only paper. Fig. 2A shows acceptor-photobleaching used purely to confirm FRET, not a photostability time course. No digitization possible. (INFO, no action.)
- **apWidth:** genuinely absent — this is a HEK293 voltage-clamp prototype paper; no optical AP trace or stated single-AP FWHM exists to extract/derive. (INFO, no action.)

## Research Papers
- Existing: 1 (Sakai 2001 origin — first entry, matches paperUrl ✓, sample "HEK 293 cells, in vitro" ✓).
- Added: 0.
- Total: 1. Judged exhaustive: VSFP1 the prototypic construct trafficked only partially to the membrane (mostly ER/Golgi-retained), gave a small ~1.8% signal, and was immediately superseded by the VSFP2.x Ci-VSP series — later papers use the successor constructs, not VSFP1. (Semantic Scholar citation API host was blocked in this environment; the prior audit ran that crawl and reached the same conclusion.)

## Cross-GEVI
- None. No other GEVI receives quantitative data from Sakai 2001 (di-4-ANEPPS is an organic dye, not a page). No cross-GEVI photobleach/apWidth panels (none exist).

## Changes Made
- voltage.custom.voltage: `[-200,-150,-100,-70,-50,0,50,100,150]` → `[-100,-70,-50,0,50,100,150]` (removed extrapolated −200/−150 mV)
- voltage.custom.deltaF: `[-2.2,-1.4,-0.5,0,0.3,1.2,2.1,3,3.8]` → `[-0.5,0,0.3,1.2,2.1,3,3.8]`
- voltage.note: added (documents ΔVm axis, linear re-referencing −70=0, extrapolation trim)
- dynamicRangeData[0].note: refined range wording ("−150→+250 mV" → measured ΔVm ≈ −125 to +210 mV; clarified x-axis is ΔVm)
- (voltage entry proofread already false; left false as required)

## Remaining Gaps
- sensitivityData, brightnessData, photostabilityData, apWidthData, photobleach, addgene: genuinely absent — the 2001 FRET prototype reports none, and it is not on Addgene. Not fabricated.

## Flags / Judgment Calls
- F-V represents a stated-linear response: points lie ON the paper's regression line (the paper characterizes VSFP1's F-V ONLY as a slope, and Fig. 3B's x-axis is ΔVm not absolute Vm, so individual scatter markers cannot be uniquely mapped to absolute voltages). Reading raw scatter and re-referencing gave noisier, steeper eyeball chords (~2.4%) than the paper's authoritative 1.8 ± 0.1%/100 mV regression — so the linear-slope representation is retained, trimmed to the measured span, rather than substituting hand-read scatter. This is the honest choice for a purely-linear sensor with a ΔVm x-axis.
- researchPapers kept at 1 (see above) — flag for the human if a broader citation crawl is desired (host was blocked here).

</details>

## GEVI Page Check: VSFP2.3
File: src/gevis/vsfp2_3.json

The page was audited 2026-06-14 and was in very good shape. I treated it as un-audited and re-verified every scientific value against the three local primary-source PDFs (Mutoh 2009, Lundby 2010, Akemann 2009) plus Mishina 2014. Nearly everything held up. One substantive addition was warranted.

## Issues Found
- **INFO**: kinetics only carried Lundby 2010 single-exponential fluorescence τ (slow-dominant fit). Mishina 2014 Table 1 publishes a full bi-exponential ON fit **with amplitude fractions** for VSFP2.3* — the rulebook's preferred form (amplitude-weighted mean, not the fast component). Added it. (Not a "fabrication/fast-component" defect in the existing entries — the Lundby single-τ is explicitly the *slow-dominant* fit, so it was already correct.)
- **INFO / Judgment call**: the Mishina 2014 Table 1 footnote does not restate the recording temperature for the VSFP2.3* row (values "From Mishina et al. 2012"). Used 35°C (the Knöpfel-lab PC12 patch-fluorometry standard, per Mutoh 2009 & Lundby 2010) with an explicit note; flagged in unresolved.
- No legacy score fields, no polarity/sign inconsistency, no missing proofread flags.

## Spectrum Check
- Status: **Real** (FRET composite). Excitation = FPbase Cerulean (donor, verbatim, peak 433), emission = FPbase Citrine (acceptor, verbatim, peak 529).

- Source: FPbase.

## F-V Curve Check
- Status: **Real (documented normalized-Boltzmann → ΔR/R rescale)**. Fig. 3B is a *normalized* (0→1) acceptor ΔF-voltage curve (paper caption: V½ = −49.5 mV, n=10) referenced to the −70 mV holding potential, NOT signed ΔF/F — so direct ΔR/R points aren't readable from the figure. The stored curve rescales that shape to the ratiometric ΔR/R anchored to the full −140→+120 mV swing = 15.2% (Mishina 2014 Table 1, verified). This follows the project's documented [[fv-normalized-boltzmann-rescale]] method.
- Verification: opened `public/fv-sources/vsfp2_3.jpg` — confirmed it IS Fig. 3B (VSFP2.3 filled circles, sigmoid, V½~−50). Stored deltaF `[−3.6,−3.2,−2.3,0,3.8,7.7,10.1,11.0,11.4,11.5,11.6]` @ `[−140..+70]`: −70=0 baseline ✓, monotonic non-decreasing ✓, positive polarity ✓, full swing = 15.2% ✓, −70→+30 step = +11.4 ✓ (matches DR ratiometric entry +11).
- Action taken: none — verified correct.
- Source: Mutoh 2009 Fig. 3B (doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0004555).

## Kinetics detail
- Lundby 2010 fluorescence single-τ at 0 mV: 46.7/49.3 (35°C), 72.0/70.6 (30°C), 85.8/137 (25°C) — **verified exact** vs the paper's Table; the paper states this single-exp "represents mainly the dominant slower kinetic component," so it is NOT a fast-component error.
- **Added** Mishina 2014 Table 1: τ1_on 3.0 ms (26.6%) + τ2_on 69.2 ms (73.4%) → amplitude-weighted mean **51.6 ms**; τoff 91.6 ms (single). For −70→+60 mV step. This bi-exponential decomposition (with published amplitudes) is the rulebook-preferred form and independently corroborates the Lundby slow-dominant value.
- All within VSD-FRET reasonable range (2–200 ms). Display picks the 35°C Lundby entry (46.7/49.3).

No mismatch to correct — noted for transparency.)_

**QA (⚠️ 2/2 checks flagged, jsonValid=True):** FAIL — both applied changes are incorrect and should be reverted or re-done. The core error is a mis-read of the ΔR/R₀ zero-reference in Akemann 2012 Fig. 3C: because experiments were run from a −70 mV holding potential, ΔR/R₀ = 0% at −70 mV by definition (Fig. 3B caption confirms this). The agent incorrectly placed −70 mV at −0.9%, which is impossible. With V½ = −69 mV and the sigmoid centred at −70 mV, the true −70→+30 mV depolarising step captures approximately the upper half of the 3.2% total range, giving ~+1.5–1.7%, not +2.9% ≈ +3%. The new value of 3 is unsupported; the note describing '−70 mV ≈ −0.9%, +30 mV ≈ +2.0%' is factually wrong. The JSON itself is syntactically valid. Recommended action: revert dynamicRangeData[1].deltaF to 3.2 (the paper's exact stated number) and update the note to explain it is the full −140→+60 mV range (per the paper's text) — or re-digitize Fig. 3C right carefully after confirming that ΔR/R₀ = 0 at −70 mV, which would yield ~+1.5–1.7% for the −70→+30 mV step (not 3).
- ❌ dynamicRangeData[1].deltaF: 3.2 → 3 (high): The agent's reasoning is incorrect. Akemann 2012 defines ΔR/R₀ relative to the −70 mV holding potential (Fig. 3B caption: 'deviation from basal fluorescence ratio (ΔR/R₀) in response to a family of step potentials … from a −70 mV holding potential'). Therefore ΔR/R₀ = 0% at −70 mV by definition — the agent's claimed reading of '−70 mV ≈ −0.9%' is impossible. With V½ = −69 mV ≈ −70 mV, the sigmoid is centred at −70 mV (ΔR/R₀ = 0) and the full range (min at −140 mV to max at +60 mV) is 3.2%. The −70→+30 mV depolarising step therefore spans from the midpoint up to near-saturation, capturing roughly the upper half of the 3.2% total range: approximately +1.5–1.7%, NOT +2.9% ≈ +3%. The new value of 3 is unsupported and too large. The old value 3.2 was at least the paper's exact stated number; replacing it with 3 (derived from a mis-read of the zero-reference) is wrong. The correct −70→+30 mV step from Fig. 3C right is ~+1.5–1.7%; the main agent should revert or re-digitize.
- ❌ dynamicRangeData[1].note: rewritten to state value is the −70→+30 mV step read off Fig. 3C with −70 mV ≈ −0.9% and +30 mV ≈ +2.0% (high): The note contains a factual error that flows from the same mis-read: '−70 mV ≈ −0.9%' cannot be correct because ΔR/R₀ is referenced to the −70 mV holding potential (R₀ is measured at −70 mV), so ΔR/R₀ must equal 0% at −70 mV. The '+30 mV ≈ +2.0%' is also overstated — the plateau of the Fig. 3C right-panel Boltzmann appears to reach only ~+1.5–1.7% above the −70 mV reference given the 3.2% total range and symmetric sigmoid around V½ ≈ −69 mV. The note's arithmetic and figure-read are both wrong and should be corrected together with the deltaF value.

- Source: FPbase (mCitrine ex peak ~516, mKate2 em peak ~633 — standard reference values; prior audit verified verbatim corr 1.00).

## F-V Curve Check
- Status: Real — read from figure, not fitted/fabricated. 12 points at real 20-mV voltage steps, −70 mV = 0, monotonic non-decreasing (positive-going), no mid-range peak/trough.
- Action taken: none to the custom curve. Verified `deltaF [-9,-8,-6,-2,0,2,5,6,6,6,6,6]` @ `[-140..+60]` directly against Fig. 2B ΔR/R0 traces (depol plateau ~+5.5-6%, hyperpol −140 ~−8/−9%, total range 15%) and against Fig. 2C Boltzmann shape (V½ −79). sourceImage present and confirmed (Read) to be the Fig. 2C normalized Boltzmann; deltaF values are the signed ΔR/R (consistent with Fig. 2B). −70→+30 step = +6% agrees with dynamicRangeData[0].
- Source: doi:10.1152/jn.00452.2012, Fig. 2C (+ Fig. 2B for signed amplitudes).

## Research Papers
- Existing: 28 papers; original (Akemann 2012) is first. List well-curated and covers the known Knöpfel-lab + Antic-lab + chi-VSFP transgenic usage through 2026. No obvious gaps; not re-run exhaustively given breadth.
- Added: 0. Total: 28.

## Changes Made
- dynamicRangeData[1].deltaF: 3.2 → 3 (source: Fig. 3C signed ΔR/R0 (%) inset; −70≈−0.9%, +30≈+2.0%). Rewrote note to state it is the −70→+30 100-mV step and keep 3.2% total range in the note.

## Remaining Gaps
- brightnessData sourceFigure absent (estimate, not from a figure) — see Flags.
- photobleach / apWidthData / photostabilityData genuinely absent (no source in either paper) — correct to omit.

## Flags / Judgment Calls
- brightnessData 1.74 is a component-FP (mCitrine donor) EC×QY proxy — the /33600 EGFP anchor gives 1.742, so the number is correct, but it has no paper figure. I did NOT add a fabricated sourceFigure and did NOT remove the entry (curator allows FRET component-FP estimates). Recorded in unresolved for the human to accept/remove.
- dynamicRangeData[1] here the −70→+30 step (~2.9%) is nearly the full 3.2% range because V½ (−69) ≈ rest and the curve saturates by +20 mV, so the correction is small but makes the label honest (100-mV step, not total range).
